revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 1]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Sugiyama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidai.jpg|280px]]
Shrine precincts {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|31|42.1|N|139|31|36.9|E}}}} | location = 208 Miyamae, Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 35.5284 | longitude = 139.5269 | deity = {{ill|Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor) debated
6th shrine of Musashi Province
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | founded = Unknown | main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | festivals = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}} }} [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the precincts}}]] '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Nishihassaku-cho, {{ill|Midori-ku, Yokohama|ja|緑区 (横浜市)}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and the sixth shrine of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}. Its historical classification under the modern shrine ranking system was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. The shrine participates in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}} of {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}}, and is counted among the "Six Great Shrines of Bushū" (武州六大明神).This includes {{ill|Ono Shrine (Tama)|ja|小野神社}} in Tama City, Tokyo; {{ill|Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|ja|二宮神社}} in Akiruno City, Tokyo; {{ill|Hikawa Shrine (Omiya)|ja|氷川神社}} in Omiya, Saitama; {{ill|Chichibu Shrine|ja|秩父神社}} in Chichibu, Saitama; {{ill|Kanazawa Shrine|ja|金鑚神社}} in Kamikawa, Saitama; and Sugiyama Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命}} === Associated Deities === * {{ill|Dainichi-ryūkei|ja|大日霊貴命}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|太田命}} According to the {{ill|Shintō-shū|ja|神道集}}, the shrine was historically called "Sugiyama Daimyōjin" (杉山大明神) with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}} as its Buddhist counterpart. Records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} note the principal deity as a standing statue of Fudō Myōō.{{Cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 83, Tsuzuki District, Sugiyama Shrine|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763987/39}}}} In {{ill|1910|ja|明治43年}}, four nearby unranked shrines, including {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}}, were merged with Sugiyama Shrine. [[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)]] == History == References to "Sugiyama Shrine" appear in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}}, while the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} lists it as the only official shrine of Tsuzuki District in Musashi Province. The shrine is also honored as one of the six "Six Shrines of Ōkunitama Shrine." The founding date is uncertain, but inscriptions on roof tiles and gravestones in the neighboring temple, Gokuraku-ji, suggest the shrine was relocated to its current location during the {{ill|Enpō era|ja|延宝}} (1673–1681). Before that, it stood 300 meters northwest at the base of a hill.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140819083559/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/musashi/bun/mus170201-01.html Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Survey of Engishiki Shrines) In {{ill|1649|ja|慶安2年}}, the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}} granted the shrine 5.6 koku of rice as stipends. In {{ill|1873|ja|明治6年}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}, and in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43年}}, it absorbed four neighboring shrines. In {{ill|1920|ja|大正9年}}, it was designated as a shrine eligible for official offerings. The current shrine building was rebuilt in {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57年}}. == Grounds == * '''Main Shrine:''' Built in {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} style. * '''Worship Hall:''' Built in {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style. * '''Chikara-ishi (Strength Stone):''' A boulder used for strength contests by the shrine's parishioners during the Edo period.Stone plaque in the precincts File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), haiden-2.jpg|Worship Hall File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho) 04.JPG|Chikara-ishi File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidaisha.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Sub-shrines == * '''Small Shrine:''' A small {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure enclosed in a thatched roof building. * '''Inari Shrine:''' A {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} shrine housed in a {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}} structure. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}: {{ill|Saitan Festival|ja|歳旦祭}} * {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}}: festival (formerly held on {{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}}) * {{ill|November 23|ja|11月23日}}: {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} * {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}}: {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓}} Additionally, the shrine participates in the "Kurayami Festival" of {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}} in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|ja|府中市}}, held from {{ill|May 3|ja|5月3日}} to {{ill|May 6|ja|5月6日}}. == Related Shrines == Sugiyama Shrine is one of many shrines of the same name in Yokohama, including: * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho)}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=179&cd=1202084&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine (Kanagawa Shrine Office)] * [http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_10.php Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Akanedai.net) [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sugiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sugiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sugiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sugiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sugiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] Tokyo|ja|府中市}}, held from {{ill|May 3|ja|5月3日}} to {{ill|May 6|ja|5月6日}}. == Related Shrines == Sugiyama Shrine is one of many shrines of the same name in Yokohama, including: * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)}} == External Links == {{Infobox religious building | name = Sugiyama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidai.jpg|280px]]
Shrine precincts {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|31|42.1|N|139|31|36.9|E}}}} | location = 208 Miyamae, Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 35.5284 | longitude = 139.5269 | deity = {{ill|Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor) debated
6th shrine of Musashi Province
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | founded = Unknown | main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | festivals = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}} }} [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the precincts}}]] '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Nishihassaku-cho, {{ill|Midori-ku, Yokohama|ja|緑区 (横浜市)}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and the sixth shrine of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}. Its historical classification under the modern shrine ranking system was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. The shrine participates in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}} of {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}}, and is counted among the "Six Great Shrines of Bushū" (武州六大明神).This includes {{ill|Ono Shrine (Tama)|ja|小野神社}} in Tama City, Tokyo; {{ill|Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|ja|二宮神社}} in Akiruno City, Tokyo; {{ill|Hikawa Shrine (Omiya)|ja|氷川神社}} in Omiya, Saitama; {{ill|Chichibu Shrine|ja|秩父神社}} in Chichibu, Saitama; {{ill|Kanazawa Shrine|ja|金鑚神社}} in Kamikawa, Saitama; and Sugiyama Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命}} === Associated Deities === * {{ill|Dainichi-ryūkei|ja|大日霊貴命}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|太田命}} According to the {{ill|Shintō-shū|ja|神道集}}, the shrine was historically called "Sugiyama Daimyōjin" (杉山大明神) with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}} as its Buddhist counterpart. Records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} note the principal deity as a standing statue of Fudō Myōō.{{Cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 83, Tsuzuki District, Sugiyama Shrine|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763987/39}}}} In {{ill|1910|ja|明治43年}}, four nearby unranked shrines, including {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}}, were merged with Sugiyama Shrine. [[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)]] == History == References to "Sugiyama Shrine" appear in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}}, while the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} lists it as the only official shrine of Tsuzuki District in Musashi Province. The shrine is also honored as one of the six "Six Shrines of Ōkunitama Shrine." The founding date is uncertain, but inscriptions on roof tiles and gravestones in the neighboring temple, Gokuraku-ji, suggest the shrine was relocated to its current location during the {{ill|Enpō era|ja|延宝}} (1673–1681). Before that, it stood 300 meters northwest at the base of a hill.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140819083559/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/musashi/bun/mus170201-01.html Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Survey of Engishiki Shrines) In {{ill|1649|ja|慶安2年}}, the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}} granted the shrine 5.6 koku of rice as stipends. In {{ill|1873|ja|明治6年}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}, and in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43年}}, it absorbed four neighboring shrines. In {{ill|1920|ja|大正9年}}, it was designated as a shrine eligible for official offerings. The current shrine building was rebuilt in {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57年}}. == Grounds == * '''Main Shrine:''' Built in {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} style. * '''Worship Hall:''' Built in {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style. * '''Chikara-ishi (Strength Stone):''' A boulder used for strength contests by the shrine's parishioners during the Edo period.Stone plaque in the precincts File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), haiden-2.jpg|Worship Hall File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho) 04.JPG|Chikara-ishi File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidaisha.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Sub-shrines == * '''Small Shrine:''' A small {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure enclosed in a thatched roof building. * '''Inari Shrine:''' A {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} shrine housed in a {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}} structure. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}: {{ill|Saitan Festival|ja|歳旦祭}} * {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}}: festival (formerly held on {{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}}) * {{ill|November 23|ja|11月23日}}: {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} * {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}}: {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓}} Additionally, the shrine participates in the "Kurayami Festival" of {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}} in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|ja|府中市}}, held from {{ill|May 3|ja|5月3日}} to {{ill|May 6|ja|5月6日}}. == Related Shrines == Sugiyama Shrine is one of many shrines of the same name in Yokohama, including: * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)}} == External Links == Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | founded = Unknown | main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | festivals = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}} }} [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the precincts}}]] '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Nishihassaku-cho, {{ill|Midori-ku, Yokohama|ja|緑区 (横浜市)}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and the sixth shrine of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}. Its historical classification under the modern shrine ranking system was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. The shrine participates in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}} of {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}}, and is counted among the "Six Great Shrines of Bushū" (武州六大明神).This includes {{ill|Ono Shrine (Tama)|ja|小野神社}} in Tama City, Tokyo; {{ill|Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|ja|二宮神社}} in Akiruno City, Tokyo; {{ill|Hikawa Shrine (Omiya)|ja|氷川神社}} in Omiya, Saitama; {{ill|Chichibu Shrine|ja|秩父神社}} in Chichibu, Saitama; {{ill|Kanazawa Shrine|ja|金鑚神社}} in Kamikawa, Saitama; and Sugiyama Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命}} === Associated Deities === * {{ill|Dainichi-ryūkei|ja|大日霊貴命}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|太田命}} According to the {{ill|Shintō-shū|ja|神道集}}, the shrine was historically called "Sugiyama Daimyōjin" (杉山大明神) with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}} as its Buddhist counterpart. Records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} note the principal deity as a standing statue of Fudō Myōō.{{Cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 83, Tsuzuki District, Sugiyama Shrine|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763987/39}}}} In {{ill|1910|ja|明治43年}}, four nearby unranked shrines, including {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}}, were merged with Sugiyama Shrine. [[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)]] == History == References to "Sugiyama Shrine" appear in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}}, while the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} lists it as the only official shrine of Tsuzuki District in Musashi Province. The shrine is also honored as one of the six "Six Shrines of Ōkunitama Shrine." The founding date is uncertain, but inscriptions on roof tiles and gravestones in the neighboring temple, Gokuraku-ji, suggest the shrine was relocated to its current location during the {{ill|Enpō era|ja|延宝}} (1673–1681). Before that, it stood 300 meters northwest at the base of a hill.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140819083559/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/musashi/bun/mus170201-01.html Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Survey of Engishiki Shrines) In {{ill|1649|ja|慶安2年}}, the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}} granted the shrine 5.6 koku of rice as stipends. In {{ill|1873|ja|明治6年}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}, and in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43年}}, it absorbed four neighboring shrines. In {{ill|1920|ja|大正9年}}, it was designated as a shrine eligible for official offerings. The current shrine building was rebuilt in {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57年}}. == Grounds == * '''Main Shrine:''' Built in {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} style. * '''Worship Hall:''' Built in {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style. * '''Chikara-ishi (Strength Stone):''' A boulder used for strength contests by the shrine's parishioners during the Edo period.Stone plaque in the precincts File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), haiden-2.jpg|Worship Hall File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho) 04.JPG|Chikara-ishi File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidaisha.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Sub-shrines == * '''Small Shrine:''' A small {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure enclosed in a thatched roof building. * '''Inari Shrine:''' A {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} shrine housed in a {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}} structure. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}: {{ill|Saitan Festival|ja|歳旦祭}} * {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}}: festival (formerly held on {{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}}) * {{ill|November 23|ja|11月23日}}: {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} * {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}}: {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓}} Additionally, the shrine participates in the "Kurayami Festival" of {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}} in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|ja|府中市}}, held from {{ill|May 3|ja|5月3日}} to {{ill|May 6|ja|5月6日}}. == Related Shrines == Sugiyama Shrine is one of many shrines of the same name in Yokohama, including: * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)}} == External Links == {{Infobox religious building | name = Sugiyama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidai.jpg|280px]]
Shrine precincts {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|31|42.1|N|139|31|36.9|E}}}} | location = 208 Miyamae, Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 35.5284 | longitude = 139.5269 | deity = {{ill|Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor) debated
6th shrine of Musashi Province
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | founded = Unknown | main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | festivals = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}} }} [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the precincts}}]] '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Nishihassaku-cho, {{ill|Midori-ku, Yokohama|ja|緑区 (横浜市)}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and the sixth shrine of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}. Its historical classification under the modern shrine ranking system was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. The shrine participates in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}} of {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}}, and is counted among the "Six Great Shrines of Bushū" (武州六大明神).This includes {{ill|Ono Shrine (Tama)|ja|小野神社}} in Tama City, Tokyo; {{ill|Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|ja|二宮神社}} in Akiruno City, Tokyo; {{ill|Hikawa Shrine (Omiya)|ja|氷川神社}} in Omiya, Saitama; {{ill|Chichibu Shrine|ja|秩父神社}} in Chichibu, Saitama; {{ill|Kanazawa Shrine|ja|金鑚神社}} in Kamikawa, Saitama; and Sugiyama Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命}} === Associated Deities === * {{ill|Dainichi-ryūkei|ja|大日霊貴命}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|太田命}} According to the {{ill|Shintō-shū|ja|神道集}}, the shrine was historically called "Sugiyama Daimyōjin" (杉山大明神) with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}} as its Buddhist counterpart. Records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} note the principal deity as a standing statue of Fudō Myōō.{{Cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 83, Tsuzuki District, Sugiyama Shrine|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763987/39}}}} In {{ill|1910|ja|明治43年}}, four nearby unranked shrines, including {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}}, were merged with Sugiyama Shrine. [[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)]] == History == References to "Sugiyama Shrine" appear in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}}, while the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} lists it as the only official shrine of Tsuzuki District in Musashi Province. The shrine is also honored as one of the six "Six Shrines of Ōkunitama Shrine." The founding date is uncertain, but inscriptions on roof tiles and gravestones in the neighboring temple, Gokuraku-ji, suggest the shrine was relocated to its current location during the {{ill|Enpō era|ja|延宝}} (1673–1681). Before that, it stood 300 meters northwest at the base of a hill.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140819083559/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/musashi/bun/mus170201-01.html Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Survey of Engishiki Shrines) In {{ill|1649|ja|慶安2年}}, the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}} granted the shrine 5.6 koku of rice as stipends. In {{ill|1873|ja|明治6年}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}, and in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43年}}, it absorbed four neighboring shrines. In {{ill|1920|ja|大正9年}}, it was designated as a shrine eligible for official offerings. The current shrine building was rebuilt in {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57年}}. == Grounds == * '''Main Shrine:''' Built in {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} style. * '''Worship Hall:''' Built in {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style. * '''Chikara-ishi (Strength Stone):''' A boulder used for strength contests by the shrine's parishioners during the Edo period.Stone plaque in the precincts Tokyo|ja|府中市}}, held from {{ill|May 3|ja|5月3日}} to {{ill|May 6|ja|5月6日}}. == Related Shrines == Sugiyama Shrine is one of many shrines of the same name in Yokohama, including: * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine {{Infobox religious building | name = Sugiyama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidai.jpg|280px]]
Shrine precincts {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|31|42.1|N|139|31|36.9|E}}}} | location = 208 Miyamae, Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 35.5284 | longitude = 139.5269 | deity = {{ill|Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor) debated
6th shrine of Musashi Province
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | founded = Unknown | main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | festivals = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}} }} [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the precincts}}]] '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Nishihassaku-cho, {{ill|Midori-ku, Yokohama|ja|緑区 (横浜市)}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and the sixth shrine of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}. Its historical classification under the modern shrine ranking system was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. The shrine participates in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}} of {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}}, and is counted among the "Six Great Shrines of Bushū" (武州六大明神).This includes {{ill|Ono Shrine (Tama)|ja|小野神社}} in Tama City, Tokyo; {{ill|Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|ja|二宮神社}} in Akiruno City, Tokyo; {{ill|Hikawa Shrine (Omiya)|ja|氷川神社}} in Omiya, Saitama; {{ill|Chichibu Shrine|ja|秩父神社}} in Chichibu, Saitama; {{ill|Kanazawa Shrine|ja|金鑚神社}} in Kamikawa, Saitama; and Sugiyama Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命}} === Associated Deities === * {{ill|Dainichi-ryūkei|ja|大日霊貴命}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|太田命}} According to the {{ill|Shintō-shū|ja|神道集}}, the shrine was historically called "Sugiyama Daimyōjin" (杉山大明神) with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}} as its Buddhist counterpart. Records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} note the principal deity as a standing statue of Fudō Myōō.{{Cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 83, Tsuzuki District, Sugiyama Shrine|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763987/39}}}} In {{ill|1910|ja|明治43年}}, four nearby unranked shrines, including {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}}, were merged with Sugiyama Shrine. [[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)]] == History == References to "Sugiyama Shrine" appear in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}}, while the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} lists it as the only official shrine of Tsuzuki District in Musashi Province. The shrine is also honored as one of the six "Six Shrines of Ōkunitama Shrine." The founding date is uncertain, but inscriptions on roof tiles and gravestones in the neighboring temple, Gokuraku-ji, suggest the shrine was relocated to its current location during the {{ill|Enpō era|ja|延宝}} (1673–1681). Before that, it stood 300 meters northwest at the base of a hill.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140819083559/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/musashi/bun/mus170201-01.html Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Survey of Engishiki Shrines) In {{ill|1649|ja|慶安2年}}, the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}} granted the shrine 5.6 koku of rice as stipends. In {{ill|1873|ja|明治6年}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}, and in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43年}}, it absorbed four neighboring shrines. In {{ill|1920|ja|大正9年}}, it was designated as a shrine eligible for official offerings. The current shrine building was rebuilt in {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57年}}. == Grounds == * '''Main Shrine:''' Built in {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} style. * '''Worship Hall:''' Built in {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style. * '''Chikara-ishi (Strength Stone):''' A boulder used for strength contests by the shrine's parishioners during the Edo period.Stone plaque in the precincts File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), haiden-2.jpg|Worship Hall File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho) 04.JPG|Chikara-ishi File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidaisha.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Sub-shrines == * '''Small Shrine:''' A small {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure enclosed in a thatched roof building. * '''Inari Shrine:''' A {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} shrine housed in a {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}} structure. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}: {{ill|Saitan Festival|ja|歳旦祭}} * {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}}: festival (formerly held on {{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}}) * {{ill|November 23|ja|11月23日}}: {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} * {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}}: {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓}} Additionally, the shrine participates in the "Kurayami Festival" of {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}} in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|ja|府中市}}, held from {{ill|May 3|ja|5月3日}} to {{ill|May 6|ja|5月6日}}. == Related Shrines == Sugiyama Shrine is one of many shrines of the same name in Yokohama, including: * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)}} == External Links == Shrine precincts {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|31|42.1|N|139|31|36.9|E}}}} | location = 208 Miyamae, Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 35.5284 | longitude = 139.5269 | deity = {{ill|Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor) debated
6th shrine of Musashi Province
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | founded = Unknown | main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | festivals = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}} }} [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the precincts}}]] '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Nishihassaku-cho, {{ill|Midori-ku, Yokohama|ja|緑区 (横浜市)}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and the sixth shrine of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}. Its historical classification under the modern shrine ranking system was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. The shrine participates in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}} of {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}}, and is counted among the "Six Great Shrines of Bushū" (武州六大明神).This includes {{ill|Ono Shrine (Tama)|ja|小野神社}} in Tama City, Tokyo; {{ill|Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|ja|二宮神社}} in Akiruno City, Tokyo; {{ill|Hikawa Shrine (Omiya)|ja|氷川神社}} in Omiya, Saitama; {{ill|Chichibu Shrine|ja|秩父神社}} in Chichibu, Saitama; {{ill|Kanazawa Shrine|ja|金鑚神社}} in Kamikawa, Saitama; and Sugiyama Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命}} === Associated Deities === * {{ill|Dainichi-ryūkei|ja|大日霊貴命}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|太田命}} According to the {{ill|Shintō-shū|ja|神道集}}, the shrine was historically called "Sugiyama Daimyōjin" (杉山大明神) with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}} as its Buddhist counterpart. Records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} note the principal deity as a standing statue of Fudō Myōō.{{Cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 83, Tsuzuki District, Sugiyama Shrine|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763987/39}}}} In {{ill|1910|ja|明治43年}}, four nearby unranked shrines, including {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}}, were merged with Sugiyama Shrine. [[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)]] == History == References to "Sugiyama Shrine" appear in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}}, while the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} lists it as the only official shrine of Tsuzuki District in Musashi Province. The shrine is also honored as one of the six "Six Shrines of Ōkunitama Shrine." The founding date is uncertain, but inscriptions on roof tiles and gravestones in the neighboring temple, Gokuraku-ji, suggest the shrine was relocated to its current location during the {{ill|Enpō era|ja|延宝}} (1673–1681). Before that, it stood 300 meters northwest at the base of a hill.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140819083559/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/musashi/bun/mus170201-01.html Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Survey of Engishiki Shrines) In {{ill|1649|ja|慶安2nd year }}, the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}} granted the shrine 5.6 koku of rice as stipends. In {{ill|1873|ja|明治6年}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}, and in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43年}}, it absorbed four neighboring shrines. In {{ill|1920|ja|大正9年}}, it was designated as a shrine eligible for official offerings. The current shrine building was rebuilt in {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57年}}. == Grounds == * '''Main Shrine:''' Built in {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} style. * '''Worship Hall:''' Built in {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style. * '''Chikara-ishi (Strength Stone):''' A boulder used for strength contests by the shrine's parishioners during the Edo period.Stone plaque in the precincts {{Infobox religious building | name = Sugiyama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidai.jpg|280px]]
Shrine precincts {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|31|42.1|N|139|31|36.9|E}}}} | location = 208 Miyamae, Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 35.5284 | longitude = 139.5269 | deity = {{ill|Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor) debated
6th shrine of Musashi Province
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | founded = Unknown | main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | festivals = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}} }} [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the precincts}}]] '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Nishihassaku-cho, {{ill|Midori-ku, Yokohama|ja|緑区 (横浜市)}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and the sixth shrine of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}. Its historical classification under the modern shrine ranking system was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. The shrine participates in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}} of {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}}, and is counted among the "Six Great Shrines of Bushū" (武州六大明神).This includes {{ill|Ono Shrine (Tama)|ja|小野神社}} in Tama City, Tokyo; {{ill|Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|ja|二宮神社}} in Akiruno City, Tokyo; {{ill|Hikawa Shrine (Omiya)|ja|氷川神社}} in Omiya, Saitama; {{ill|Chichibu Shrine|ja|秩父神社}} in Chichibu, Saitama; {{ill|Kanazawa Shrine|ja|金鑚神社}} in Kamikawa, Saitama; and Sugiyama Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命}} === Associated Deities === * {{ill|Dainichi-ryūkei|ja|大日霊貴命}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|太田命}} According to the {{ill|Shintō-shū|ja|神道集}}, the shrine was historically called "Sugiyama Daimyōjin" (杉山大明神) with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}} as its Buddhist counterpart. Records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} note the principal deity as a standing statue of Fudō Myōō.{{Cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 83, Tsuzuki District, Sugiyama Shrine|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763987/39}}}} In {{ill|1910|ja|明治43rd year }}, four nearby unranked shrines, including {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}}, were merged with Sugiyama Shrine. [[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)]] == History == References to "Sugiyama Shrine" appear in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}}, while the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} lists it as the only official shrine of Tsuzuki District in Musashi Province. The shrine is also honored as one of the six "Six Shrines of Ōkunitama Shrine." The founding date is uncertain, but inscriptions on roof tiles and gravestones in the neighboring temple, Gokuraku-ji, suggest the shrine was relocated to its current location during the {{ill|Enpō era|ja|延宝}} (1673–1681). Before that, it stood 300 meters northwest at the base of a hill.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140819083559/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/musashi/bun/mus170201-01.html Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Survey of Engishiki Shrines) In {{ill|1649|ja|慶安2nd year }}, the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}} granted the shrine 5.6 koku of rice as stipends. In {{ill|1873|ja|明治6年}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}, and in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43rd year }}, it absorbed four neighboring shrines. In {{ill|1920|ja|大正9年}}, it was designated as a shrine eligible for official offerings. The current shrine building was rebuilt in {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57年}}. == Grounds == * '''Main Shrine:''' Built in {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} style. * '''Worship Hall:''' Built in {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style. * '''Chikara-ishi (Strength Stone):''' A boulder used for strength contests by the shrine's parishioners during the Edo period.Stone plaque in the precincts File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), haiden-2.jpg|Worship Hall File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho) 04.JPG|Chikara-ishi File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidaisha.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Sub-shrines == * '''Small Shrine:''' A small {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure enclosed in a thatched roof building. * '''Inari Shrine:''' A {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} shrine housed in a {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}} structure. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}: {{ill|Saitan Festival|ja|歳旦祭}} * {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}}: festival (formerly held on {{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}}) * {{ill|November 23|ja|11月23日}}: {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} * {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}}: {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓}} Additionally, the shrine participates in the "Kurayami Festival" of {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}} in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|ja|府中市}}, held from {{ill|May 3|ja|5月3日}} to {{ill|May 6|ja|5月6日}}. == Related Shrines == Sugiyama Shrine is one of many shrines of the same name in Yokohama, including: * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)}} == External Links == {{Infobox religious building | name = Sugiyama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidai.jpg|280px]]
Shrine precincts {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|31|42.1|N|139|31|36.9|E}}}} | location = 208 Miyamae, Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 35.5284 | longitude = 139.5269 | deity = {{ill|Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor) debated
6th shrine of Musashi Province
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | founded = Unknown | main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | festivals = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}} }} [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the precincts}}]] '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Nishihassaku-cho, {{ill|Midori-ku, Yokohama|ja|緑区 (横浜市)}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and the sixth shrine of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}. Its historical classification under the modern shrine ranking system was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. The shrine participates in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}} of {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}}, and is counted among the "Six Great Shrines of Bushū" (武州六大明神).This includes {{ill|Ono Shrine (Tama)|ja|小野神社}} in Tama City, Tokyo; {{ill|Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|ja|二宮神社}} in Akiruno City, Tokyo; {{ill|Hikawa Shrine (Omiya)|ja|氷川神社}} in Omiya, Saitama; {{ill|Chichibu Shrine|ja|秩父神社}} in Chichibu, Saitama; {{ill|Kanazawa Shrine|ja|金鑚神社}} in Kamikawa, Saitama; and Sugiyama Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命}} === Associated Deities === * {{ill|Dainichi-ryūkei|ja|大日霊貴命}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|太田命}} According to the {{ill|Shintō-shū|ja|神道集}}, the shrine was historically called "Sugiyama Daimyōjin" (杉山大明神) with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}} as its Buddhist counterpart. Records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} note the principal deity as a standing statue of Fudō Myōō.{{Cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 83, Tsuzuki District, Sugiyama Shrine|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763987/39}}}} In {{ill|1910|ja|明治43rd year }}, four nearby unranked shrines, including {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}}, were merged with Sugiyama Shrine. [[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)]] == History == References to "Sugiyama Shrine" appear in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}}, while the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} lists it as the only official shrine of Tsuzuki District in Musashi Province. The shrine is also honored as one of the six "Six Shrines of Ōkunitama Shrine." The founding date is uncertain, but inscriptions on roof tiles and gravestones in the neighboring temple, Gokuraku-ji, suggest the shrine was relocated to its current location during the {{ill|Enpō era|ja|延宝}} (1673–1681). Before that, it stood 300 meters northwest at the base of a hill.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140819083559/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/musashi/bun/mus170201-01.html Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Survey of Engishiki Shrines) In {{ill|1649|ja|慶安2nd year }}, the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}} granted the shrine 5.6 koku of rice as stipends. In {{ill|1873|ja|明治6年}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}, and in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43rd year }}, it absorbed four neighboring shrines. In {{ill|1920|ja|大正9年}}, it was designated as a shrine eligible for official offerings. The current shrine building was rebuilt in {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57th year }}. == Grounds == * '''Main Shrine:''' Built in {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} style. * '''Worship Hall:''' Built in {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style. * '''Chikara-ishi (Strength Stone):''' A boulder used for strength contests by the shrine's parishioners during the Edo period.Stone plaque in the precincts File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), haiden-2.jpg|Worship Hall File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho) 04.JPG|Chikara-ishi File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidaisha.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Sub-shrines == * '''Small Shrine:''' A small {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure enclosed in a thatched roof building. * '''Inari Shrine:''' A {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} shrine housed in a {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}} structure. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}: {{ill|Saitan Festival|ja|歳旦祭}} * {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}}: festival (formerly held on {{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}}) * {{ill|November 23|ja|11月23日}}: {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} * {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}}: {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓}} Additionally, the shrine participates in the "Kurayami Festival" of {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}} in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|ja|府中市}}, held from {{ill|May 3|ja|5月3日}} to {{ill|May 6|ja|5月6日}}. == Related Shrines == Sugiyama Shrine is one of many shrines of the same name in Yokohama, including: * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)}} == External Links == Shrine precincts {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|31|42.1|N|139|31|36.9|E}}}} | location = 208 Miyamae, Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 35.5284 | longitude = 139.5269 | deity = {{ill|Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor) debated

Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | founded = Unknown | main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | festivals = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}} }} [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the precincts}}]] '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Nishihassaku-cho, {{ill|Midori-ku, Yokohama|ja|緑区 (横浜市)}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and the sixth shrine of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}. Its historical classification under the modern shrine ranking system was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. The shrine participates in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}} of {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}}, and is counted among the "Six Great Shrines of Bushū" (武州六大明神).This includes {{ill|Ono Shrine (Tama)|ja|小野神社}} in Tama City, Tokyo; {{ill|Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|ja|二宮神社}} in Akiruno City, Tokyo; {{ill|Hikawa Shrine (Omiya)|ja|氷川神社}} in Omiya, Saitama; {{ill|Chichibu Shrine|ja|秩父神社}} in Chichibu, Saitama; {{ill|Kanazawa Shrine|ja|金鑚神社}} in Kamikawa, Saitama; and Sugiyama Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命}} === Associated Deities === * {{ill|Dainichi-ryūkei|ja|大日霊貴命}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|太田命}} According to the {{ill|Shintō-shū|ja|神道集}}, the shrine was historically called "Sugiyama Daimyōjin" (杉山大明神) with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}} as its Buddhist counterpart. Records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} note the principal deity as a standing statue of Fudō Myōō.{{Cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 83, Tsuzuki District, Sugiyama Shrine|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763987/39}}}} In {{ill|1910|ja|明治43rd year }}, four nearby unranked shrines, including {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}}, were merged with Sugiyama Shrine. [[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)]] == History == References to "Sugiyama Shrine" appear in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}}, while the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} lists it as the only official shrine of Tsuzuki District in Musashi Province. The shrine is also honored as one of the six "Six Shrines of Ōkunitama Shrine." The founding date is uncertain, but inscriptions on roof tiles and gravestones in the neighboring temple, Gokuraku-ji, suggest the shrine was relocated to its current location during the {{ill|Enpō era|ja|延宝}} (1673–1681). Before that, it stood 300 meters northwest at the base of a hill.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140819083559/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/musashi/bun/mus170201-01.html Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Survey of Engishiki Shrines) In {{ill|1649|ja|慶安2nd year }}, the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}} granted the shrine 5.6 koku of rice as stipends. In {{ill|1873|ja|明治6年}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}, and in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43rd year }}, it absorbed four neighboring shrines. In {{ill|1920|ja|大正9th year }}, it was designated as a shrine eligible for official offerings. {{Infobox religious building | name = Sugiyama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidai.jpg|280px]]
Shrine precincts {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|31|42.1|N|139|31|36.9|E}}}} | location = 208 Miyamae, Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 35.5284 | longitude = 139.5269 | deity = {{ill|Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor) debated
6th shrine of Musashi Province
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | founded = Unknown | main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | festivals = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}} }} [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the precincts}}]] '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Nishihassaku-cho, {{ill|Midori-ku, Yokohama|ja|緑区 (横浜市)}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and the sixth shrine of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}. Its historical classification under the modern shrine ranking system was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. The shrine participates in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}} of {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}}, and is counted among the "Six Great Shrines of Bushū" (武州六大明神).This includes {{ill|Ono Shrine (Tama)|ja|小野神社}} in Tama City, Tokyo; {{ill|Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|ja|二宮神社}} in Akiruno City, Tokyo; {{ill|Hikawa Shrine (Omiya)|ja|氷川神社}} in Omiya, Saitama; {{ill|Chichibu Shrine|ja|秩父神社}} in Chichibu, Saitama; {{ill|Kanazawa Shrine|ja|金鑚神社}} in Kamikawa, Saitama; and Sugiyama Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命}} === Associated Deities === * {{ill|Dainichi-ryūkei|ja|大日霊貴命}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|太田命}} According to the {{ill|Shintō-shū|ja|神道集}}, the shrine was historically called "Sugiyama Daimyōjin" (杉山大明神) with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}} as its Buddhist counterpart. Records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} note the principal deity as a standing statue of Fudō Myōō.{{Cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 83, Tsuzuki District, Sugiyama Shrine|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763987/39}}}} In {{ill|1910|ja|明治43rd year }}, four nearby unranked shrines, including {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}}, were merged with Sugiyama Shrine. [[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)]] == History == References to "Sugiyama Shrine" appear in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}}, while the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} lists it as the only official shrine of Tsuzuki District in Musashi Province. The shrine is also honored as one of the six "Six Shrines of Ōkunitama Shrine." The founding date is uncertain, but inscriptions on roof tiles and gravestones in the neighboring temple, Gokuraku-ji, suggest the shrine was relocated to its current location during the {{ill|Enpō era|ja|延宝}} (1673–1681). Before that, it stood 300 meters northwest at the base of a hill.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140819083559/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/musashi/bun/mus170201-01.html Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Survey of Engishiki Shrines) In {{ill|1649|ja|慶安2nd year }}, the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}} granted the shrine 5.6 koku of rice as stipends. In {{ill|1873|ja|明治6年}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}, and in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43rd year }}, it absorbed four neighboring shrines. In {{ill|1920|ja|大正9th year }}, it was designated as a shrine eligible for official offerings. The current shrine building was rebuilt in {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57th year }}. == Grounds == * '''Main Shrine:''' Built in {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} style. * '''Worship Hall:''' Built in {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style. * '''Chikara-ishi (Strength Stone):''' A boulder used for strength contests by the shrine's parishioners during the Edo period.Stone plaque in the precincts File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), haiden-2.jpg|Worship Hall File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho) 04.JPG|Chikara-ishi File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidaisha.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Sub-shrines == * '''Small Shrine:''' A small {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure enclosed in a thatched roof building. * '''Inari Shrine:''' A {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} shrine housed in a {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}} structure. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}: {{ill|Saitan Festival|ja|歳旦祭}} * {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}}: festival (formerly held on {{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}}) * {{ill|November 23|ja|11月23日}}: {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} * {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}}: {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓}} Additionally, the shrine participates in the "Kurayami Festival" of {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}} in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|ja|府中市}}, held from {{ill|May 3|ja|5月3日}} to {{ill|May 6|ja|5月6日}}. == Related Shrines == Sugiyama Shrine is one of many shrines of the same name in Yokohama, including: * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)}} == External Links == {{Infobox religious building | name = Sugiyama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidai.jpg|280px]]
Shrine precincts {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|31|42.1|N|139|31|36.9|E}}}} | location = 208 Miyamae, Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 35.5284 | longitude = 139.5269 | deity = {{ill|Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor) debated
6th shrine of Musashi Province
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | founded = Unknown | main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | festivals = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}} }} [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the precincts}}]] '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Nishihassaku-cho, {{ill|Midori-ku, Yokohama|ja|緑区 (横浜市)}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and the sixth shrine of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}. Its historical classification under the modern shrine ranking system was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. The shrine participates in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}} of {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}}, and is counted among the "Six Great Shrines of Bushū" (武州六大明神).This includes {{ill|Ono Shrine (Tama)|ja|小野神社}} in Tama City, Tokyo; {{ill|Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|ja|二宮神社}} in Akiruno City, Tokyo; {{ill|Hikawa Shrine (Omiya)|ja|氷川神社}} in Omiya, Saitama; {{ill|Chichibu Shrine|ja|秩父神社}} in Chichibu, Saitama; {{ill|Kanazawa Shrine|ja|金鑚神社}} in Kamikawa, Saitama; and Sugiyama Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命}} === Associated Deities === * {{ill|Dainichi-ryūkei|ja|大日霊貴命}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|太田命}} According to the {{ill|Shintō-shū|ja|神道集}}, the shrine was historically called "Sugiyama Daimyōjin" (杉山大明神) with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}} as its Buddhist counterpart. Records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} note the principal deity as a standing statue of Fudō Myōō.{{Cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 83, Tsuzuki District, Sugiyama Shrine|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763987/39}}}} In {{ill|1910|ja|明治43rd year }}, four nearby unranked shrines, including {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}}, were merged with Sugiyama Shrine. [[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)]] == History == References to "Sugiyama Shrine" appear in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}}, while the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} lists it as the only official shrine of Tsuzuki District in Musashi Province. The shrine is also honored as one of the six "Six Shrines of Ōkunitama Shrine." The founding date is uncertain, but inscriptions on roof tiles and gravestones in the neighboring temple, Gokuraku-ji, suggest the shrine was relocated to its current location during the {{ill|Enpō era|ja|延宝}} (1673–1681). Before that, it stood 300 meters northwest at the base of a hill.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140819083559/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/musashi/bun/mus170201-01.html Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Survey of Engishiki Shrines) In {{ill|1649|ja|慶安2nd year }}, the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}} granted the shrine 5.6 koku of rice as stipends. In {{ill|1873|ja|明治6年}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}, and in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43rd year }}, it absorbed four neighboring shrines. In {{ill|1920|ja|大正9th year }}, it was designated as a shrine eligible for official offerings. The current shrine building was rebuilt in {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57th year }}. == Grounds == * '''Main Shrine:''' Built in {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} style. * '''Worship Hall:''' Built in {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style. * '''Chikara-ishi (Strength Stone):''' A boulder used for strength contests by the shrine's parishioners during the Edo period.Stone plaque in the precincts File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), haiden-2.jpg|Worship Hall File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho) 04.JPG|Chikara-ishi File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidaisha.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Sub-shrines == * '''Small Shrine:''' A small {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure enclosed in a thatched roof building. * '''Inari Shrine:''' A {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} shrine housed in a {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}} structure. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}: {{ill|Saitan Festival|ja|歳旦祭}} * {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}}: festival (formerly held on {{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}}) * {{ill|November 23|ja|11月23日}}: {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} * {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日}}: {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓}} Additionally, the shrine participates in the "Kurayami Festival" of {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}} in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|ja|府中市}}, held from {{ill|May 3|ja|5月3日}} to {{ill|May 6|ja|5月6日}}. == Related Shrines == Sugiyama Shrine is one of many shrines of the same name in Yokohama, including: * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)}} == External Links == {{Infobox religious building | name = Sugiyama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidai.jpg|280px]]
Shrine precincts {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|31|42.1|N|139|31|36.9|E}}}} | location = 208 Miyamae, Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 35.5284 | longitude = 139.5269 | deity = {{ill|Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor) debated
6th shrine of Musashi Province
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | founded = Unknown | main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | festivals = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日}} }} [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the precincts}}]] '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Nishihassaku-cho, {{ill|Midori-ku, Yokohama|ja|緑区 (横浜市)}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and the sixth shrine of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}. Its historical classification under the modern shrine ranking system was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. The shrine participates in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}} of {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}}, and is counted among the "Six Great Shrines of Bushū" (武州六大明神).This includes {{ill|Ono Shrine (Tama)|ja|小野神社}} in Tama City, Tokyo; {{ill|Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|ja|二宮神社}} in Akiruno City, Tokyo; {{ill|Hikawa Shrine (Omiya)|ja|氷川神社}} in Omiya, Saitama; {{ill|Chichibu Shrine|ja|秩父神社}} in Chichibu, Saitama; {{ill|Kanazawa Shrine|ja|金鑚神社}} in Kamikawa, Saitama; and Sugiyama Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命}} === Associated Deities === * {{ill|Dainichi-ryūkei|ja|大日霊貴命}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|太田命}} According to the {{ill|Shintō-shū|ja|神道集}}, the shrine was historically called "Sugiyama Daimyōjin" (杉山大明神) with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}} as its Buddhist counterpart. Records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} note the principal deity as a standing statue of Fudō Myōō.{{Cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 83, Tsuzuki District, Sugiyama Shrine|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763987/39}}}} In {{ill|1910|ja|明治43rd year }}, four nearby unranked shrines, including {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}}, were merged with Sugiyama Shrine. == History == References to "Sugiyama Shrine" appear in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}}, while the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} lists it as the only official shrine of Tsuzuki District in Musashi Province. The shrine is also honored as one of the six "Six Shrines of Ōkunitama Shrine." The founding date is uncertain, but inscriptions on roof tiles and gravestones in the neighboring temple, Gokuraku-ji, suggest the shrine was relocated to its current location during the {{ill|Enpō era|ja|延宝}} (1673–1681). Before that, it stood 300 meters northwest at the base of a hill.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140819083559/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/musashi/bun/mus170201-01.html Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Survey of Engishiki Shrines) In {{ill|1649|ja|慶安2nd year }}, the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}} granted the shrine 5.6 koku of rice as stipends. In {{ill|1873|ja|明治6年}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}, and in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43rd year }}, it absorbed four neighboring shrines. In {{ill|1920|ja|大正9th year }}, it was designated as a shrine eligible for official offerings. The current shrine building was rebuilt in {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57th year }}. == Grounds == * '''Main Shrine:''' Built in {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} style. * '''Worship Hall:''' Built in {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style. * '''Chikara-ishi (Strength Stone):''' A boulder used for strength contests by the shrine's parishioners during the Edo period.Stone plaque in the precincts Tokyo|ja|府中市}}, held from {{ill|May 3|ja|5月3日}} to {{ill|May 6|ja|5月6日}}. == Related Shrines == Sugiyama Shrine is one of many shrines of the same name in Yokohama, including: * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)}} == External Links == Its historical classification under the modern shrine ranking system was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}}. The shrine participates in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|12=draft}} of {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社|12=draft}}, and is counted among the "Six Great Shrines of Bushū" (武州六大明神).This includes {{ill|Ono Shrine (Tama)|ja|小野神社|12=draft}} in Tama City, Tokyo; {{ill|Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|ja|二宮神社|12=draft}} in Akiruno City, Tokyo; {{ill|Hikawa Shrine (Omiya)|ja|氷川神社|12=draft}} in Omiya, Saitama; {{ill|Chichibu Shrine|ja|秩父神社|12=draft}} in Chichibu, Saitama; {{ill|Kanazawa Shrine|ja|金鑚神社|12=draft}} in Kamikawa, Saitama; and Sugiyama Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|12=draft}} === Associated Deities === * {{ill|Dainichi-ryūkei|ja|大日霊貴命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|太田命|12=draft}} According to the {{ill|Shintō-shū|ja|神道集|12=draft}}, the shrine was historically called "Sugiyama Daimyōjin" (杉山大明神) with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|12=draft}} as its Buddhist counterpart. Records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}} note the principal deity as a standing statue of Fudō Myōō.{{Cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 83, Tsuzuki District, Sugiyama Shrine|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763987/39}}}} In {{ill|1910|ja|明治43rd year|12=draft}}, four nearby unranked shrines, including {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社|12=draft}}, were merged with Sugiyama Shrine. == History == References to "Sugiyama Shrine" appear in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|12=draft}}, while the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}} lists it as the only official shrine of Tsuzuki District in Musashi Province. The shrine is also honored as one of the six "Six Shrines of Ōkunitama Shrine." The founding date is uncertain, but inscriptions on roof tiles and gravestones in the neighboring temple, Gokuraku-ji, suggest the shrine was relocated to its current location during the {{ill|Enpō era|ja|延宝|12=draft}} (1673–1681). Before that, it stood 300 meters northwest at the base of a hill.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140819083559/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/musashi/bun/mus170201-01.html Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Survey of Engishiki Shrines) In {{ill|1649|ja|慶安2nd year|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|12=draft}} granted the shrine 5.6 koku of rice as stipends. In {{ill|1873|ja|明治6年|12=draft}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}}, and in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43rd year|12=draft}}, it absorbed four neighboring shrines. In {{ill|1920|ja|大正9th year|12=draft}}, it was designated as a shrine eligible for official offerings. The current shrine building was rebuilt in {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57th year|12=draft}}. == Grounds == * '''Main Shrine:''' Built in {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} style. * '''Worship Hall:''' Built in {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|12=draft}} style. * '''Chikara-ishi (Strength Stone):''' A boulder used for strength contests by the shrine's parishioners during the Edo period.Stone plaque in the precincts File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), haiden-2.jpg|Worship Hall File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho) 04.JPG|Chikara-ishi File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidaisha.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Sub-shrines == * '''Small Shrine:''' A small {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} structure enclosed in a thatched roof building. * '''Inari Shrine:''' A {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} shrine housed in a {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|12=draft}} structure. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|12=draft}}: {{ill|Saitan Festival|ja|歳旦祭|12=draft}} * {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日|12=draft}}: festival (formerly held on {{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日|12=draft}}) * {{ill|November 23|ja|11月23日|12=draft}}: {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft}} * {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日|12=draft}}: {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓|12=draft}} Additionally, the shrine participates in the "Kurayami Festival" of {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|ja|府中市|12=draft}}, held from {{ill|May 3|ja|5月3日|12=draft}} to {{ill|May 6|ja|5月6日|12=draft}}. == Related Shrines == Sugiyama Shrine is one of many shrines of the same name in Yokohama, including: * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|12=draft}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho)}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=179&cd=1202084&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine (Kanagawa Shrine Office)] * [http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_10.php Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Akanedai.net) {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Sugiyama Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Yokohama shrines]] [[Category:Musashi Province shrines]] [[Category:Midori Ward, Yokohama]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)]] [[sn:Sugiyama Shrine]] [[en:Sugiyama Shrine]] [[ja:杉山神社 (緑区西八朔町)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sugiyama Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Sugiyama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidai.jpg|280px]]
Shrine precincts {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|31|42.1|N|139|31|36.9|E}}}} | location = 208 Miyamae, Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 35.5284 | longitude = 139.5269 | deity = {{ill|Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|12=draft}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} (minor) debated
6th shrine of Musashi Province
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} | founded = Unknown | main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} | festivals = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日|12=draft}} }} [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the precincts}}]] '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in Nishihassaku-cho, {{ill|Midori-ku, Yokohama|ja|緑区 (横浜市)|12=draft}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} and the sixth shrine of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国|12=draft}}. Its historical classification under the modern shrine ranking system was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}}. The shrine participates in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|12=draft}} of {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社|12=draft}}, and is counted among the "Six Great Shrines of Bushū" (武州六大明神).This includes {{ill|Ono Shrine (Tama)|ja|小野神社|12=draft}} in Tama City, Tokyo; {{ill|Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|ja|二宮神社|12=draft}} in Akiruno City, Tokyo; {{ill|Hikawa Shrine (Omiya)|ja|氷川神社|12=draft}} in Omiya, Saitama; {{ill|Chichibu Shrine|ja|秩父神社|12=draft}} in Chichibu, Saitama; {{ill|Kanazawa Shrine|ja|金鑚神社|12=draft}} in Kamikawa, Saitama; and Sugiyama Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|12=draft}} === Associated Deities === * {{ill|Dainichi-ryūkei|ja|大日霊貴命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|太田命|12=draft}} According to the {{ill|Shintō-shū|ja|神道集|12=draft}}, the shrine was historically called "Sugiyama Daimyōjin" (杉山大明神) with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|12=draft}} as its Buddhist counterpart. Records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}} note the principal deity as a standing statue of Fudō Myōō.{{Cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 83, Tsuzuki District, Sugiyama Shrine|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763987/39}}}} In {{ill|1910|ja|明治43rd year|12=draft}}, four nearby unranked shrines, including {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社|12=draft}}, were merged with Sugiyama Shrine. == History == References to "Sugiyama Shrine" appear in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|12=draft}}, while the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}} lists it as the only official shrine of Tsuzuki District in Musashi Province. The shrine is also honored as one of the six "Six Shrines of Ōkunitama Shrine." The founding date is uncertain, but inscriptions on roof tiles and gravestones in the neighboring temple, Gokuraku-ji, suggest the shrine was relocated to its current location during the {{ill|Enpō era|ja|延宝|12=draft}} (1673–1681). Before that, it stood 300 meters northwest at the base of a hill.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140819083559/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/musashi/bun/mus170201-01.html Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Survey of Engishiki Shrines) In {{ill|1649|ja|慶安2nd year|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|12=draft}} granted the shrine 5.6 koku of rice as stipends. In {{ill|1873|ja|明治6年|12=draft}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}}, and in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43rd year|12=draft}}, it absorbed four neighboring shrines. In {{ill|1920|ja|大正9th year|12=draft}}, it was designated as a shrine eligible for official offerings. The current shrine building was rebuilt in {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57th year|12=draft}}. == Grounds == * '''Main Shrine:''' Built in {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} style. * '''Worship Hall:''' Built in {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|12=draft}} style. * '''Chikara-ishi (Strength Stone):''' A boulder used for strength contests by the shrine's parishioners during the Edo period.Stone plaque in the precincts File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), haiden-2.jpg|Worship Hall File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho) 04.JPG|Chikara-ishi File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidaisha.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Sub-shrines == * '''Small Shrine:''' A small {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} structure enclosed in a thatched roof building. * '''Inari Shrine:''' A {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} shrine housed in a {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|12=draft}} structure. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|12=draft}}: {{ill|Saitan Festival|ja|歳旦祭|12=draft}} * {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日|12=draft}}: festival (formerly held on {{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日|12=draft}}) * {{ill|November 23|ja|11月23日|12=draft}}: {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft}} * {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日|12=draft}}: {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓|12=draft}} Additionally, the shrine participates in the "Kurayami Festival" of {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|ja|府中市|12=draft}}, held from {{ill|May 3|ja|5月3日|12=draft}} to {{ill|May 6|ja|5月6日|12=draft}}. == Related Shrines == Sugiyama Shrine is one of many shrines of the same name in Yokohama, including: * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|12=draft}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho)}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=179&cd=1202084&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine (Kanagawa Shrine Office)] * [http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_10.php Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Akanedai.net) {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Sugiyama Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Yokohama shrines]] [[Category:Musashi Province shrines]] [[Category:Midori Ward, Yokohama]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)]] [[sn:Sugiyama Shrine]] [[en:Sugiyama Shrine]] [[ja:杉山神社 (緑区西八朔町)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sugiyama Shrine}}

of {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|ja|府中市|12=draft|12=draft}}, held from {{ill|May 3|ja|5月3日|12=draft|12=draft}} to {{ill|May 6|ja|5月6日|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Related Shrines == Sugiyama Shrine is one of many shrines of the same name in Yokohama, including: * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == revision.comment: Text replacement - "不動明王|" to "不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|"
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Sugiyama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidai.jpg|280px]]
Shrine precincts {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|31|42.1|N|139|31|36.9|E}}}} | location = 208 Miyamae, Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 35.5284 | longitude = 139.5269 | deity = {{ill|Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|12=draft|12=draft}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} (minor) debated
6th shrine of Musashi Province
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} | founded = Unknown | main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} | festivals = {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日|12=draft|12=draft}} }} [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the precincts}}]] '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Nishihassaku-cho, {{ill|Midori-ku, Yokohama|ja|緑区 (横浜市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and the sixth shrine of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its historical classification under the modern shrine Ranking system was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine participates in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, and is counted among the "Six Great Shrines of Bushū" (武州六大明神).This includes {{ill|Ono Shrine (Tama)|ja|小野神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in Tama City, Tokyo; {{ill|Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|ja|二宮神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in Akiruno City, Tokyo; {{ill|Hikawa Shrine (Omiya)|ja|氷川神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in Omiya, Saitama; {{ill|Chichibu Shrine|ja|秩父神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in Chichibu, Saitama; {{ill|Kanazawa Shrine|ja|金鑚神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in Kamikawa, Saitama; and Sugiyama Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|12=draft|12=draft}} === Associated Deities === * {{ill|Dainichi-ryūkei|ja|大日霊貴命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|太田命|12=draft|12=draft}} According to the {{ill|Shintō-shū|ja|神道集|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was historically called "Sugiyama Daimyōjin" (杉山大明神) with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|12=draft|12=draft}} as its Buddhist counterpart. Records from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}} note the principal deity as a standing statue of Fudō Myōō.{{Cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 83, Tsuzuki District, Sugiyama Shrine|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763987/39}}}} In {{ill|1910|ja|明治43rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}, four nearby unRanked shrines, including {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, were merged with Sugiyama Shrine. == History == References to "Sugiyama Shrine" appear in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, while the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}} lists it as the only official shrine of Tsuzuki District in Musashi Province. The shrine is also honored as one of the six "Six Shrines of Ōkunitama Shrine." The founding date is uncertain, but inscriptions on roof tiles and gravestones in the neighboring temple, Gokuraku-ji, suggest the shrine was relocated to its current location during the {{ill|Enpō era|ja|延宝|12=draft|12=draft}} (1673–1681). Before that, it stood 300 meters northwest at the base of a hill.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140819083559/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/musashi/bun/mus170201-01.html Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Survey of Engishiki Shrines) In {{ill|1649|ja|慶安2nd year|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|12=draft|12=draft}} granted the shrine 5.6 koku of rice as stipends. In {{ill|1873|ja|明治6年|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}}, and in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}, it absorbed four neighboring shrines. In {{ill|1920|ja|大正9th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was designated as a shrine eligible for official offerings. The current shrine building was rebuilt in {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57th year|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Grounds == * '''Main Shrine:''' Built in {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} style. * '''Worship Hall:''' Built in {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|12=draft|12=draft}} style. * '''Chikara-ishi (Strength Stone):''' A boulder used for strength contests by the shrine's parishioners during the Edo period.Stone plaque in the precincts File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), haiden-2.jpg|Worship Hall File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho) 04.JPG|Chikara-ishi File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidaisha.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Sub-shrines == * '''Small Shrine:''' A small {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} structure enclosed in a thatched roof building. * '''Inari Shrine:''' A {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} shrine housed in a {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|12=draft|12=draft}} structure. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|Saitan Festival|ja|歳旦祭|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|October 1|ja|10月1日|12=draft|12=draft}}: festival (formerly held on {{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|November 23|ja|11月23日|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|December 22|ja|12月22日|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓|12=draft|12=draft}} Additionally, the shrine participates in the "Kurayami Festival" of {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|ja|府中市|12=draft|12=draft}}, held from {{ill|May 3|ja|5月3日|12=draft|12=draft}} to {{ill|May 6|ja|5月6日|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Related Shrines == Sugiyama Shrine is one of many shrines of the same name in Yokohama, including: * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho)}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=179&cd=1202084&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine (Kanagawa Shrine Office)] * [http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_10.php Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Akanedai.net) {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Sugiyama Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Yokohama shrines]] [[Category:Musashi Province shrines]] [[Category:Midori Ward, Yokohama]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)]] [[sn:Sugiyama Shrine]] [[en:Sugiyama Shrine]] [[ja:杉山神社 (緑区西八朔町)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sugiyama Shrine}}
