{{Infobox religious building | name = Sugiyama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidai.jpg|280px]]
Shrine precincts {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|31|42.1|N|139|31|36.9|E}}}} | location = 208 Miyamae, Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 35.5284 | longitude = 139.5269 | deity = {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor) debated
6th shrine of Musashi Province
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | founded = Unknown | main_building_style = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festivals = [[:en:October 1|October 1]] }} [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the precincts}}]] '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Nishihassaku-cho, [[:en:Midori-ku, Yokohama|Midori-ku, Yokohama]], Kanagawa Prefecture. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and the sixth shrine of [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]. Its historical classification under the modern shrine Ranking system was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. The shrine participates in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|qq=}} of [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]], and is counted among the "Six Great Shrines of Bushū" (武州六大明神).This includes {{ill|Ono Shrine (Tama)|ja|小野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Tama)|qq=}} in Tama City, Tokyo; {{ill|Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|ja|二宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|qq=}} in Akiruno City, Tokyo; [[:en:Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|Hikawa Shrine (Omiya)]] in Omiya, Saitama; [[:en:Chichibu Shrine|Chichibu Shrine]] in Chichibu, Saitama; [[:en:Kanasana Shrine|Kanazawa Shrine]] in Kamikawa, Saitama; and Sugiyama Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=}} === Associated Deities === * {{ill|Dainichi-ryūkei|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi-ryūkei|qq=}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|太田命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} According to the [[:en:Shintōshū|Shintō-shū]], the shrine was historically called "Sugiyama Daimyōjin" (杉山大明神) with [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] as its Buddhist counterpart. Records from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] note the principal deity as a standing statue of Fudō Myōō.{{Cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 83, Tsuzuki District, Sugiyama Shrine|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763987/39}}}} In {{ill|1910|ja|明治43年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1910|qq=}}, four nearby unranked shrines, including [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]], were merged with Sugiyama Shrine. == History == References to "Sugiyama Shrine" appear in the [[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihon Kōki]], while the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} lists it as the only official shrine of Tsuzuki District in Musashi Province. The shrine is also honored as one of the six "Six Shrines of Ōkunitama Shrine." The founding date is uncertain, but inscriptions on roof tiles and gravestones in the neighboring temple, Gokuraku-ji, suggest the shrine was relocated to its current location during the [[:en:Enpō|Enpō era]] (1673–1681). Before that, it stood 300 meters northwest at the base of a hill.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140819083559/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/musashi/bun/mus170201-01.html Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Survey of Shikinaisha) In {{ill|1649|ja|慶安2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1649|qq=}}, the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}} granted the shrine 5.6 koku of rice as stipends. In {{ill|1873|ja|明治6年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1873|qq=}}, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}, and in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1910|qq=}}, it absorbed four neighboring shrines. In {{ill|1920|ja|大正9年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1920|qq=}}, it was designated as a shrine eligible for official offerings. The current shrine building was rebuilt in {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1982|qq=}}. == Grounds == * '''Main Shrine:''' Built in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * '''Worship Hall:''' Built in [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style. * '''Chikara-ishi (Strength Stone):''' A boulder used for strength contests by the shrine's parishioners during the Edo period.Stone plaque in the precincts File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), haiden-2.jpg|Worship Hall File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho) 04.JPG|Chikara-ishi File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidaisha.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Sub-shrines == * '''Small Shrine:''' A small [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] structure enclosed in a thatched roof building. * '''Inari Shrine:''' A [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] shrine housed in a {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|qq=}} structure. == Festivals == * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan Festival|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:October 1|October 1]]: festival (formerly held on [[:en:September 15|September 15]]) * [[:en:November 23|November 23]]: [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] * [[:en:December 22|December 22]]: [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Ōharae]] Additionally, the shrine participates in the "Kurayami Festival" of [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]] in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|ja|府中市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuchū, Tokyo|qq=}}, held from [[:en:May 3|May 3]] to [[:en:May 6|May 6]]. == Related Shrines == Sugiyama Shrine is one of many shrines of the same name in Yokohama, including: * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|qq=}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho)}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=179&cd=1202084&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine (Kanagawa Shrine Office)] * [http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_10.php Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] contests by the shrine's parishioners during the Edo period.Stone plaque in the precincts File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), haiden-2.jpg|Worship Hall File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho) 04.JPG|Chikara-ishi File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidaisha.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Sub-shrines == * '''Small Shrine:''' A small [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] structure enclosed in a thatched roof building. * '''Inari Shrine:''' A [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] shrine housed in a {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|qq=}} structure. == Festivals == * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan Festival|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:October 1|October 1]]: festival (formerly held on [[:en:September 15|September 15]]) * [[:en:November 23|November 23]]: [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] * [[:en:December 22|December 22]]: [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Ōharae]] Additionally, the shrine participates in the "Kurayami Festival" of [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]] in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|ja|府中市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuchū, Tokyo|qq=}}, held from [[:en:May 3|May 3]] to [[:en:May 6|May 6]]. == Related Shrines == Sugiyama Shrine is one of many shrines of the same name in Yokohama, including: * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|qq=}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho)}} * revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:横浜市緑区の歴史
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Sugiyama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidai.jpg|280px]]
Shrine precincts {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|31|42.1|N|139|31|36.9|E}}}} | location = 208 Miyamae, Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 35.5284 | longitude = 139.5269 | deity = {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor) debated
6th shrine of Musashi Province
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | founded = Unknown | main_building_style = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festivals = [[:en:October 1|October 1]] }} [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the precincts}}]] '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Nishihassaku-cho, [[:en:Midori-ku, Yokohama|Midori-ku, Yokohama]], Kanagawa Prefecture. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and the sixth shrine of [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]. Its historical classification under the modern shrine Ranking system was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. The shrine participates in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|qq=}} of [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]], and is counted among the "Six Great Shrines of Bushū" (武州六大明神).This includes {{ill|Ono Shrine (Tama)|ja|小野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Tama)|qq=}} in Tama City, Tokyo; {{ill|Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|ja|二宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|qq=}} in Akiruno City, Tokyo; [[:en:Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|Hikawa Shrine (Omiya)]] in Omiya, Saitama; [[:en:Chichibu Shrine|Chichibu Shrine]] in Chichibu, Saitama; [[:en:Kanasana Shrine|Kanazawa Shrine]] in Kamikawa, Saitama; and Sugiyama Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=}} === Associated Deities === * {{ill|Dainichi-ryūkei|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi-ryūkei|qq=}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|太田命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} According to the [[:en:Shintōshū|Shintō-shū]], the shrine was historically called "Sugiyama Daimyōjin" (杉山大明神) with [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] as its Buddhist counterpart. Records from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] note the principal deity as a standing statue of Fudō Myōō.{{Cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 83, Tsuzuki District, Sugiyama Shrine|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763987/39}}}} In {{ill|1910|ja|明治43年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1910|qq=}}, four nearby unranked shrines, including [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]], were merged with Sugiyama Shrine. == History == References to "Sugiyama Shrine" appear in the [[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihon Kōki]], while the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} lists it as the only official shrine of Tsuzuki District in Musashi Province. The shrine is also honored as one of the six "Six Shrines of Ōkunitama Shrine." The founding date is uncertain, but inscriptions on roof tiles and gravestones in the neighboring temple, Gokuraku-ji, suggest the shrine was relocated to its current location during the [[:en:Enpō|Enpō era]] (1673–1681). Before that, it stood 300 meters northwest at the base of a hill.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140819083559/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/musashi/bun/mus170201-01.html Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Survey of Shikinaisha) In {{ill|1649|ja|慶安2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1649|qq=}}, the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}} granted the shrine 5.6 koku of rice as stipends. In {{ill|1873|ja|明治6年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1873|qq=}}, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}, and in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1910|qq=}}, it absorbed four neighboring shrines. In {{ill|1920|ja|大正9年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1920|qq=}}, it was designated as a shrine eligible for official offerings. The current shrine building was rebuilt in {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1982|qq=}}. == Grounds == * '''Main Shrine:''' Built in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * '''Worship Hall:''' Built in [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style. * '''Chikara-ishi (Strength Stone):''' A boulder used for strength contests by the shrine's parishioners during the Edo period.Stone plaque in the precincts File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), haiden-2.jpg|Worship Hall File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho) 04.JPG|Chikara-ishi File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidaisha.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Sub-shrines == * '''Small Shrine:''' A small [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] structure enclosed in a thatched roof building. * '''Inari Shrine:''' A [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] shrine housed in a {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|qq=}} structure. == Festivals == * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan Festival|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:October 1|October 1]]: festival (formerly held on [[:en:September 15|September 15]]) * [[:en:November 23|November 23]]: [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] * [[:en:December 22|December 22]]: [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Ōharae]] Additionally, the shrine participates in the "Kurayami Festival" of [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]] in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|ja|府中市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuchū, Tokyo|qq=}}, held from [[:en:May 3|May 3]] to [[:en:May 6|May 6]]. == Related Shrines == Sugiyama Shrine is one of many shrines of the same name in Yokohama, including: * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|qq=}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho)}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=179&cd=1202084&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine (Kanagawa Shrine Office)] * [http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_10.php Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Akanedai.net) {{translated page|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)|version=103331334|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] }} [[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)]] [[sn:Sugiyama Shrine]] [[en:Sugiyama Shrine]] [[ja:杉山神社 (緑区西八朔町)]] [[Category:Sugiyama Shrines]] [[Category:横浜市の神社]] [[Category:横浜市緑区の建築物]] [[Category:横浜市緑区の歴史]]

[[:en:September 15|September 15]]) * [[:en:November 23|November 23]]: [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] * [[:en:December 22|December 22]]: [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Ōharae]] Additionally, the shrine participates in the "Kurayami Festival" of [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]] in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|ja|府中市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuchū, Tokyo|qq=}}, held from [[:en:May 3|May 3]] to [[:en:May 6|May 6]]. == Related Shrines == Sugiyama Shrine is one of many shrines of the same name in Yokohama, including: * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|qq=}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho)}} * revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Sugiyama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidai.jpg|280px]]
Shrine precincts {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|31|42.1|N|139|31|36.9|E}}}} | location = 208 Miyamae, Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 35.5284 | longitude = 139.5269 | deity = {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor) debated
6th shrine of Musashi Province
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | founded = Unknown | main_building_style = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festivals = [[:en:October 1|October 1]] }} [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the precincts}}]] '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Nishihassaku-cho, [[:en:Midori-ku, Yokohama|Midori-ku, Yokohama]], Kanagawa Prefecture. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and the sixth shrine of [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]. Its historical classification under the modern shrine Ranking system was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. The shrine participates in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|qq=}} of [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]], and is counted among the "Six Great Shrines of Bushū" (武州六大明神).This includes {{ill|Ono Shrine (Tama)|ja|小野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Tama)|qq=}} in Tama City, Tokyo; {{ill|Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|ja|二宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|qq=}} in Akiruno City, Tokyo; [[:en:Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|Hikawa Shrine (Omiya)]] in Omiya, Saitama; [[:en:Chichibu Shrine|Chichibu Shrine]] in Chichibu, Saitama; [[:en:Kanasana Shrine|Kanazawa Shrine]] in Kamikawa, Saitama; and Sugiyama Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=}} === Associated Deities === * {{ill|Dainichi-ryūkei|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi-ryūkei|qq=}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|太田命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} According to the [[:en:Shintōshū|Shintō-shū]], the shrine was historically called "Sugiyama Daimyōjin" (杉山大明神) with [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] as its Buddhist counterpart. Records from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] note the principal deity as a standing statue of Fudō Myōō.{{Cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 83, Tsuzuki District, Sugiyama Shrine|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763987/39}}}} In {{ill|1910|ja|明治43年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1910|qq=}}, four nearby unranked shrines, including [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]], were merged with Sugiyama Shrine. == History == References to "Sugiyama Shrine" appear in the [[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihon Kōki]], while the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} lists it as the only official shrine of Tsuzuki District in Musashi Province. The shrine is also honored as one of the six "Six Shrines of Ōkunitama Shrine." The founding date is uncertain, but inscriptions on roof tiles and gravestones in the neighboring temple, Gokuraku-ji, suggest the shrine was relocated to its current location during the [[:en:Enpō|Enpō era]] (1673–1681). Before that, it stood 300 meters northwest at the base of a hill.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140819083559/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/musashi/bun/mus170201-01.html Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Survey of Shikinaisha) In {{ill|1649|ja|慶安2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1649|qq=}}, the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}} granted the shrine 5.6 koku of rice as stipends. In {{ill|1873|ja|明治6年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1873|qq=}}, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}, and in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1910|qq=}}, it absorbed four neighboring shrines. In {{ill|1920|ja|大正9年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1920|qq=}}, it was designated as a shrine eligible for official offerings. The current shrine building was rebuilt in {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1982|qq=}}. == Grounds == * '''Main Shrine:''' Built in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * '''Worship Hall:''' Built in [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style. * '''Chikara-ishi (Strength Stone):''' A boulder used for strength contests by the shrine's parishioners during the Edo period.Stone plaque in the precincts File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), haiden-2.jpg|Worship Hall File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho) 04.JPG|Chikara-ishi File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidaisha.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Sub-shrines == * '''Small Shrine:''' A small [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] structure enclosed in a thatched roof building. * '''Inari Shrine:''' A [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] shrine housed in a {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|qq=}} structure. == Festivals == * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan Festival|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:October 1|October 1]]: festival (formerly held on [[:en:September 15|September 15]]) * [[:en:November 23|November 23]]: [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] * [[:en:December 22|December 22]]: [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Ōharae]] Additionally, the shrine participates in the "Kurayami Festival" of [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]] in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|ja|府中市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuchū, Tokyo|qq=}}, held from [[:en:May 3|May 3]] to [[:en:May 6|May 6]]. == Related Shrines == Sugiyama Shrine is one of many shrines of the same name in Yokohama, including: * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|qq=}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho)}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=179&cd=1202084&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine (Kanagawa Shrine Office)] * [http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_10.php Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Akanedai.net) {{translated page|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)|version=103331334|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] }} [[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)]] [[sn:Sugiyama Shrine]] [[en:Sugiyama Shrine]] [[ja:杉山神社 (緑区西八朔町)]] [[Category:Sugiyama Shrines]] [[Category:横浜市の神社]] [[Category:横浜市緑区の建築物]] (横浜市港北区新吉田町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|qq=}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho)}} * revision.comment: Bot: update category → History of Midori-ku, Yokohama
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Sugiyama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidai.jpg|280px]]
Shrine precincts {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|31|42.1|N|139|31|36.9|E}}}} | location = 208 Miyamae, Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 35.5284 | longitude = 139.5269 | deity = {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor) debated
6th shrine of Musashi Province
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | founded = Unknown | main_building_style = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festivals = [[:en:October 1|October 1]] }} [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the precincts}}]] '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Nishihassaku-cho, [[:en:Midori-ku, Yokohama|Midori-ku, Yokohama]], Kanagawa Prefecture. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and the sixth shrine of [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]. Its historical classification under the modern shrine Ranking system was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. The shrine participates in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|qq=}} of [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]], and is counted among the "Six Great Shrines of Bushū" (武州六大明神).This includes {{ill|Ono Shrine (Tama)|ja|小野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Tama)|qq=}} in Tama City, Tokyo; {{ill|Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|ja|二宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|qq=}} in Akiruno City, Tokyo; [[:en:Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|Hikawa Shrine (Omiya)]] in Omiya, Saitama; [[:en:Chichibu Shrine|Chichibu Shrine]] in Chichibu, Saitama; [[:en:Kanasana Shrine|Kanazawa Shrine]] in Kamikawa, Saitama; and Sugiyama Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=}} === Associated Deities === * {{ill|Dainichi-ryūkei|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi-ryūkei|qq=}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|太田命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} According to the [[:en:Shintōshū|Shintō-shū]], the shrine was historically called "Sugiyama Daimyōjin" (杉山大明神) with [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] as its Buddhist counterpart. Records from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] note the principal deity as a standing statue of Fudō Myōō.{{Cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 83, Tsuzuki District, Sugiyama Shrine|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763987/39}}}} In {{ill|1910|ja|明治43年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1910|qq=}}, four nearby unranked shrines, including [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]], were merged with Sugiyama Shrine. == History == References to "Sugiyama Shrine" appear in the [[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihon Kōki]], while the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} lists it as the only official shrine of Tsuzuki District in Musashi Province. The shrine is also honored as one of the six "Six Shrines of Ōkunitama Shrine." The founding date is uncertain, but inscriptions on roof tiles and gravestones in the neighboring temple, Gokuraku-ji, suggest the shrine was relocated to its current location during the [[:en:Enpō|Enpō era]] (1673–1681). Before that, it stood 300 meters northwest at the base of a hill.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140819083559/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/musashi/bun/mus170201-01.html Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Survey of Shikinaisha) In {{ill|1649|ja|慶安2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1649|qq=}}, the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}} granted the shrine 5.6 koku of rice as stipends. In {{ill|1873|ja|明治6年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1873|qq=}}, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}, and in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1910|qq=}}, it absorbed four neighboring shrines. In {{ill|1920|ja|大正9年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1920|qq=}}, it was designated as a shrine eligible for official offerings. The current shrine building was rebuilt in {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1982|qq=}}. == Grounds == * '''Main Shrine:''' Built in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * '''Worship Hall:''' Built in [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style. * '''Chikara-ishi (Strength Stone):''' A boulder used for strength contests by the shrine's parishioners during the Edo period.Stone plaque in the precincts File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), haiden-2.jpg|Worship Hall File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho) 04.JPG|Chikara-ishi File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidaisha.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Sub-shrines == * '''Small Shrine:''' A small [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] structure enclosed in a thatched roof building. * '''Inari Shrine:''' A [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] shrine housed in a {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|qq=}} structure. == Festivals == * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan Festival|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:October 1|October 1]]: festival (formerly held on [[:en:September 15|September 15]]) * [[:en:November 23|November 23]]: [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] * [[:en:December 22|December 22]]: [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Ōharae]] Additionally, the shrine participates in the "Kurayami Festival" of [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]] in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|ja|府中市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuchū, Tokyo|qq=}}, held from [[:en:May 3|May 3]] to [[:en:May 6|May 6]]. == Related Shrines == Sugiyama Shrine is one of many shrines of the same name in Yokohama, including: * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|qq=}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho)}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=179&cd=1202084&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine (Kanagawa Shrine Office)] * [http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_10.php Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Akanedai.net) {{translated page|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)|version=103331334|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] }} [[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)]] [[sn:Sugiyama Shrine]] [[en:Sugiyama Shrine]] [[ja:杉山神社 (緑区西八朔町)]] [[Category:Sugiyama Shrines]] [[Category:横浜市の神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Midori-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Midori-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]]

[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=179&cd=1202084&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine (Kanagawa Shrine Office)] * [http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_10.php Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] Prefecture]] }} [[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)]] [[sn:Sugiyama Shrine]] [[ja:杉山神社 (緑区西八朔町)]] [[Category:Sugiyama Shrines]] [[Category:横浜市の神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Midori-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Midori-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Sugiyama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidai.jpg|280px]]
Shrine precincts {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|31|42.1|N|139|31|36.9|E}}}} | location = 208 Miyamae, Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 35.5284 | longitude = 139.5269 | deity = {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor) debated
6th shrine of Musashi Province
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | founded = Unknown | main_building_style = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festivals = [[:en:October 1|October 1]] }} [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the precincts}}]] '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Nishihassaku-cho, [[:en:Midori-ku, Yokohama|Midori-ku, Yokohama]], Kanagawa Prefecture. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and the sixth shrine of [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]. Its historical classification under the modern shrine Ranking system was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. The shrine participates in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|qq=}} of [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]], and is counted among the "Six Great Shrines of Bushū" (武州六大明神).This includes {{ill|Ono Shrine (Tama)|ja|小野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Tama)|qq=}} in Tama City, Tokyo; {{ill|Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|ja|二宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|qq=}} in Akiruno City, Tokyo; [[:en:Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|Hikawa Shrine (Omiya)]] in Omiya, Saitama; [[:en:Chichibu Shrine|Chichibu Shrine]] in Chichibu, Saitama; [[:en:Kanasana Shrine|Kanazawa Shrine]] in Kamikawa, Saitama; and Sugiyama Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=}} === Associated Deities === * {{ill|Dainichi-ryūkei|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi-ryūkei|qq=}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|太田命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} According to the [[:en:Shintōshū|Shintō-shū]], the shrine was historically called "Sugiyama Daimyōjin" (杉山大明神) with [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] as its Buddhist counterpart. Records from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] note the principal deity as a standing statue of Fudō Myōō.{{Cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 83, Tsuzuki District, Sugiyama Shrine|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763987/39}}}} In {{ill|1910|ja|明治43年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1910|qq=}}, four nearby unranked shrines, including [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]], were merged with Sugiyama Shrine. == History == References to "Sugiyama Shrine" appear in the [[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihon Kōki]], while the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} lists it as the only official shrine of Tsuzuki District in Musashi Province. The shrine is also honored as one of the six "Six Shrines of Ōkunitama Shrine." The founding date is uncertain, but inscriptions on roof tiles and gravestones in the neighboring temple, Gokuraku-ji, suggest the shrine was relocated to its current location during the [[:en:Enpō|Enpō era]] (1673–1681). Before that, it stood 300 meters northwest at the base of a hill.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140819083559/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/musashi/bun/mus170201-01.html Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Survey of Shikinaisha) In {{ill|1649|ja|慶安2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1649|qq=}}, the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}} granted the shrine 5.6 koku of rice as stipends. In {{ill|1873|ja|明治6年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1873|qq=}}, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}, and in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1910|qq=}}, it absorbed four neighboring shrines. In {{ill|1920|ja|大正9年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1920|qq=}}, it was designated as a shrine eligible for official offerings. The current shrine building was rebuilt in {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1982|qq=}}. == Grounds == * '''Main Shrine:''' Built in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * '''Worship Hall:''' Built in [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style. * '''Chikara-ishi (Strength Stone):''' A boulder used for strength contests by the shrine's parishioners during the Edo period.Stone plaque in the precincts File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), haiden-2.jpg|Worship Hall File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho) 04.JPG|Chikara-ishi File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidaisha.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Sub-shrines == * '''Small Shrine:''' A small [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] structure enclosed in a thatched roof building. * '''Inari Shrine:''' A [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] shrine housed in a {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|qq=}} structure. == Festivals == * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan Festival|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:October 1|October 1]]: festival (formerly held on [[:en:September 15|September 15]]) * [[:en:November 23|November 23]]: [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] * [[:en:December 22|December 22]]: [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Ōharae]] Additionally, the shrine participates in the "Kurayami Festival" of [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]] in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|ja|府中市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuchū, Tokyo|qq=}}, held from [[:en:May 3|May 3]] to [[:en:May 6|May 6]]. == Related Shrines == Sugiyama Shrine is one of many shrines of the same name in Yokohama, including: * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|qq=}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho)}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=179&cd=1202084&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine (Kanagawa Shrine Office)] * [http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_10.php Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Akanedai.net) {{translated page|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)|version=103331334|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] }} [[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)]] [[sn:Sugiyama Shrine]] [[ja:杉山神社 (緑区西八朔町)]] [[Category:Sugiyama Shrines]] [[Category:横浜市の神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Midori-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History {{Infobox religious building | name = Sugiyama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidai.jpg|280px]]
Shrine precincts {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|31|42.1|N|139|31|36.9|E}}}} | location = 208 Miyamae, Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 35.5284 | longitude = 139.5269 | deity = {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor) debated
6th shrine of Musashi Province
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | founded = Unknown | main_building_style = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festivals = [[:en:October 1|October 1]] }} [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the precincts}}]] '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Nishihassaku-cho, [[:en:Midori-ku, Yokohama|Midori-ku, Yokohama]], Kanagawa Prefecture. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and the sixth shrine of [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]. Its historical classification under the modern shrine Ranking system was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. The shrine participates in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|qq=}} of [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]], and is counted among the "Six Great Shrines of Bushū" (武州六大明神).This includes {{ill|Ono Shrine (Tama)|ja|小野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Tama)|qq=}} in Tama City, Tokyo; {{ill|Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|ja|二宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|qq=}} in Akiruno City, Tokyo; [[:en:Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|Hikawa Shrine (Omiya)]] in Omiya, Saitama; [[:en:Chichibu Shrine|Chichibu Shrine]] in Chichibu, Saitama; [[:en:Kanasana Shrine|Kanazawa Shrine]] in Kamikawa, Saitama; and Sugiyama Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=}} === Associated Deities === * {{ill|Dainichi-ryūkei|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi-ryūkei|qq=}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|太田命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} According to the [[:en:Shintōshū|Shintō-shū]], the shrine was historically called "Sugiyama Daimyōjin" (杉山大明神) with [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] as its Buddhist counterpart. Records from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] note the principal deity as a standing statue of Fudō Myōō.{{Cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 83, Tsuzuki District, Sugiyama Shrine|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763987/39}}}} In {{ill|1910|ja|明治43年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1910|qq=}}, four nearby unranked shrines, including [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]], were merged with Sugiyama Shrine. == History == References to "Sugiyama Shrine" appear in the [[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihon Kōki]], while the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} lists it as the only official shrine of Tsuzuki District in Musashi Province. The shrine is also honored as one of the six "Six Shrines of Ōkunitama Shrine." The founding date is uncertain, but inscriptions on roof tiles and gravestones in the neighboring temple, Gokuraku-ji, suggest the shrine was relocated to its current location during the [[:en:Enpō|Enpō era]] (1673–1681). Before that, it stood 300 meters northwest at the base of a hill.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140819083559/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/musashi/bun/mus170201-01.html Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Survey of Shikinaisha) In {{ill|1649|ja|慶安2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1649|qq=}}, the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}} granted the shrine 5.6 koku of rice as stipends. In {{ill|1873|ja|明治6年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1873|qq=}}, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}, and in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1910|qq=}}, it absorbed four neighboring shrines. In {{ill|1920|ja|大正9年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1920|qq=}}, it was designated as a shrine eligible for official offerings. The current shrine building was rebuilt in {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1982|qq=}}. == Grounds == * '''Main Shrine:''' Built in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * '''Worship Hall:''' Built in [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style. * '''Chikara-ishi (Strength Stone):''' A boulder used for strength contests by the shrine's parishioners during the Edo period.Stone plaque in the precincts File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), haiden-2.jpg|Worship Hall File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho) 04.JPG|Chikara-ishi File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidaisha.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Sub-shrines == * '''Small Shrine:''' A small [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] structure enclosed in a thatched roof building. * '''Inari Shrine:''' A [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] shrine housed in a {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|qq=}} structure. == Festivals == * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan Festival|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:October 1|October 1]]: festival (formerly held on [[:en:September 15|September 15]]) * [[:en:November 23|November 23]]: [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] * [[:en:December 22|December 22]]: [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Ōharae]] Additionally, the shrine participates in the "Kurayami Festival" of [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]] in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|ja|府中市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuchū, Tokyo|qq=}}, held from [[:en:May (横浜市都筑区中川)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|qq=}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho)}} * {{Infobox religious building | name = Sugiyama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidai.jpg|280px]]
Shrine precincts {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|31|42.1|N|139|31|36.9|E}}}} | location = 208 Miyamae, Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 35.5284 | longitude = 139.5269 | deity = {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (minor) debated
6th shrine of Musashi Province
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | founded = Unknown | main_building_style = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festivals = [[:en:October 1|October 1]] }} [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the precincts}}]] '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Nishihassaku-cho, [[:en:Midori-ku, Yokohama|Midori-ku, Yokohama]], Kanagawa Prefecture. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} and the sixth shrine of [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]. Its historical classification under the modern shrine Ranking system was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. The shrine participates in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|qq=}} of [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]], and is counted among the "Six Great Shrines of Bushū" (武州六大明神).This includes {{ill|Ono Shrine (Tama)|ja|小野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Tama)|qq=}} in Tama City, Tokyo; {{ill|Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|ja|二宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|qq=}} in Akiruno City, Tokyo; [[:en:Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|Hikawa Shrine (Omiya)]] in Omiya, Saitama; [[:en:Chichibu Shrine|Chichibu Shrine]] in Chichibu, Saitama; [[:en:Kanasana Shrine|Kanazawa Shrine]] in Kamikawa, Saitama; and Sugiyama Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神}} === Associated Deities === * {{ill|Dainichi-ryūkei|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi-ryūkei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|太田命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} According to the [[:en:Shintōshū|Shintō-shū]], the shrine was historically called "Sugiyama Daimyōjin" (杉山大明神) with [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] as its Buddhist counterpart. Records from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] note the principal deity as a standing statue of Fudō Myōō.{{Cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 83, Tsuzuki District, Sugiyama Shrine|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763987/39}}}} In {{ill|1910|ja|明治43年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1910|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1910年}}, four nearby unranked shrines, including [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]], were merged with Sugiyama Shrine. == History == References to "Sugiyama Shrine" appear in the [[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihon Kōki]], while the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} lists it as the only official shrine of Tsuzuki District in Musashi Province. The shrine is also honored as one of the six "Six Shrines of Ōkunitama Shrine." The founding date is uncertain, but inscriptions on roof tiles and gravestones in the neighboring temple, Gokuraku-ji, suggest the shrine was relocated to its current location during the [[:en:Enpō|Enpō era]] (1673–1681). Before that, it stood 300 meters northwest at the base of a hill.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140819083559/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/musashi/bun/mus170201-01.html Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Survey of Shikinaisha) In {{ill|1649|ja|慶安2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1649|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} granted the shrine 5.6 koku of rice as stipends. In {{ill|1873|ja|明治6年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1873|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1873年}}, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}, and in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1910|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1910年}}, it absorbed four neighboring shrines. In {{ill|1920|ja|大正9年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1920|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1920年}}, it was designated as a shrine eligible for official offerings. The current shrine building was rebuilt in {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1982|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1982年}}. == Grounds == * '''Main Shrine:''' Built in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * '''Worship Hall:''' Built in [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style. * '''Chikara-ishi (Strength Stone):''' A boulder used for strength contests by the shrine's parishioners during the Edo period.Stone plaque in the precincts File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), haiden-2.jpg|Worship Hall File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho) 04.JPG|Chikara-ishi File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidaisha.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Sub-shrines == * '''Small Shrine:''' A small [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] structure enclosed in a thatched roof building. * '''Inari Shrine:''' A [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] shrine housed in a {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|qq=}} structure. == Festivals == * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan Festival|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:October 1|October 1]]: festival (formerly held on [[:en:September 15|September 15]]) * [[:en:November 23|November 23]]: [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] * [[:en:December 22|December 22]]: [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Ōharae]] Additionally, the shrine participates in the "Kurayami Festival" of [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]] in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|ja|府中市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuchū, Tokyo|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, held from [[:en:May 3|May 3]] to [[:en:May 6|May 6]]. == Related Shrines == Sugiyama Shrine is one of many shrines of the same name in Yokohama, including: * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|qq=}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho)}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=179&cd=1202084&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine (Kanagawa Shrine Office)] * [http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_10.php Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Sugiyama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidai.jpg|280px]]
Shrine precincts {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|31|42.1|N|139|31|36.9|E}}}} | location = 208 Miyamae, Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 35.5284 | longitude = 139.5269 | deity = {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (minor) debated
6th shrine of Musashi Province
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | founded = Unknown | main_building_style = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festivals = [[:en:October 1|October 1]] }} [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the precincts}}]] '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Nishihassaku-cho, [[:en:Midori-ku, Yokohama|Midori-ku, Yokohama]], Kanagawa Prefecture. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} and the sixth shrine of [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]. Its historical classification under the modern shrine Ranking system was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. The shrine participates in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|qq=}} of [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]], and is counted among the "Six Great Shrines of Bushū" (武州六大明神).This includes {{ill|Ono Shrine (Tama)|ja|小野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Tama)|qq=}} in Tama City, Tokyo; {{ill|Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|ja|二宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|qq=}} in Akiruno City, Tokyo; [[:en:Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|Hikawa Shrine (Omiya)]] in Omiya, Saitama; [[:en:Chichibu Shrine|Chichibu Shrine]] in Chichibu, Saitama; [[:en:Kanasana Shrine|Kanazawa Shrine]] in Kamikawa, Saitama; and Sugiyama Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神}} === Associated Deities === * {{ill|Dainichi-ryūkei|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi-ryūkei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|太田命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} According to the [[:en:Shintōshū|Shintō-shū]], the shrine was historically called "Sugiyama Daimyōjin" (杉山大明神) with [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] as its Buddhist counterpart. Records from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] note the principal deity as a standing statue of Fudō Myōō.{{Cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 83, Tsuzuki District, Sugiyama Shrine|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763987/39}}}} In {{ill|1910|ja|明治43年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1910|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1910年}}, four nearby unranked shrines, including [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]], were merged with Sugiyama Shrine. == History == References to "Sugiyama Shrine" appear in the [[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihon Kōki]], while the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} lists it as the only official shrine of Tsuzuki District in Musashi Province. The shrine is also honored as one of the six "Six Shrines of Ōkunitama Shrine." The founding date is uncertain, but inscriptions on roof tiles and gravestones in the neighboring temple, Gokuraku-ji, suggest the shrine was relocated to its current location during the [[:en:Enpō|Enpō era]] (1673–1681). Before that, it stood 300 meters northwest at the base of a hill.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140819083559/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/musashi/bun/mus170201-01.html Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Survey of Shikinaisha) In [[:en:1649|1649]], the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} granted the shrine 5.6 koku of rice as stipends. In [[:en:1873|1873]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}, and in {{ill|1910|ja|明治43年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1910|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1910年}}, it absorbed four neighboring shrines. In {{ill|1920|ja|大正9年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1920|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1920年}}, it was designated as a shrine eligible for official offerings. The current shrine building was rebuilt in {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1982|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1982年}}. == Grounds == * '''Main Shrine:''' Built in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * '''Worship Hall:''' Built in [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style. * '''Chikara-ishi (Strength Stone):''' A boulder used for strength contests by the shrine's parishioners during the Edo period.Stone plaque in the precincts File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), haiden-2.jpg|Worship Hall File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho) 04.JPG|Chikara-ishi File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidaisha.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Sub-shrines == * '''Small Shrine:''' A small [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] structure enclosed in a thatched roof building. * '''Inari Shrine:''' A [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] shrine housed in a {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|qq=}} structure. == Festivals == * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan Festival|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:October 1|October 1]]: festival (formerly held on [[:en:September 15|September 15]]) * [[:en:November 23|November 23]]: [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] * [[:en:December 22|December 22]]: [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Ōharae]] Additionally, the shrine participates in the "Kurayami Festival" of [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]] in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|ja|府中市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuchū, Tokyo|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, held from [[:en:May 3|May 3]] to [[:en:May 6|May 6]]. == Related Shrines == Sugiyama Shrine is one of many shrines of the same name in Yokohama, including: * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|qq=}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho)}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=179&cd=1202084&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine (Kanagawa Shrine Office)] * [http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_10.php Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Akanedai.net) {{translated page|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)|version=103331334|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] }} [[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)]] [[sn:Sugiyama Shrine]] [[ja:杉山神社 (緑区西八朔町)]] [[Category:Sugiyama Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Midori-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Midori-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine,
