It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and the sixth shrine of [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]. Its historical classification under the modern shrine Ranking system was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine participates in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|qq=|1=Kurayami Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265601}} of [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]], and is counted among the "Six Great Shrines of Bushū" (武州六大明神).This includes {{ill|Ono Shrine (Tama)|ja|小野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Tama)|qq=|1=Ono Shrine (Tama)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464223}} in Tama City, Tokyo; {{ill|Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|ja|二宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|qq=|1=Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371507}} in Akiruno City, Tokyo; [[:en:Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|Hikawa Shrine (Omiya)]] in Omiya, Saitama; [[:en:Chichibu Shrine|Chichibu Shrine]] in Chichibu, Saitama; [[:en:Kanasana Shrine|Kanazawa Shrine]] in Kamikawa, Saitama; and Sugiyama Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}} === Associated Deities === * {{ill|Dainichi-ryūkei|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi-ryūkei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|太田命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} According to the [[:en:Shintōshū|Shintō-shū]], the shrine was historically called "Sugiyama Daimyōjin" (杉山大明神) with [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] as its Buddhist counterpart. Records from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] note the principal deity as a standing statue of Fudō Myōō.{{Cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 83, Tsuzuki District, Sugiyama Shrine|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763987/39}}}} In [[:en:1910|1910]], four nearby unranked shrines, including [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]], were merged with Sugiyama Shrine. == History == References to "Sugiyama Shrine" appear in the [[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihon Kōki]], while the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} lists it as the only official shrine of Tsuzuki District in Musashi Province. The shrine is also honored as one of the six "Six Shrines of Ōkunitama Shrine." The founding date is uncertain, but inscriptions on roof tiles and gravestones in the neighboring temple, Gokuraku-ji, suggest the shrine was relocated to its current location during the [[:en:Enpō|Enpō era]] (1673–1681). Before that, it stood 300 meters northwest at the base of a hill.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140819083559/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/musashi/bun/mus170201-01.html Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Survey of Shikinaisha) In [[:en:1649|1649]], the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} granted the shrine 5.6 koku of rice as stipends. In [[:en:1873|1873]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in [[:en:1910|1910]], it absorbed four neighboring shrines. In [[:en:1920|1920]], it was designated as a shrine eligible for official offerings. The current shrine building was rebuilt in [[:en:1982|1982]]. == Grounds == * '''Main Shrine:''' Built in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * '''Worship Hall:''' Built in [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style. * '''Chikara-ishi (Strength Stone):''' A boulder used for strength contests by the shrine's parishioners during the Edo period.Stone plaque in the precincts [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] structure enclosed in a thatched roof building. * '''Inari Shrine:''' A [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] shrine housed in a [[sn:commons:Category:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho)]][[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11521945]] Wikidata name: Sugiyama jinjya [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Sugiyama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), haiden-2.jpg | location = 208 Miyamae, Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 35.5284 | longitude = 139.5269 | deity = {{ill|Isotakeru|WD=Q10881556}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor) debated
6th shrine of Musashi Province
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = Unknown | main_building_style = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festivals = [[:en:October 1|October 1]] | location = {{ill|Midori-ku|WD=Q1203373}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.52836111111111|N|139.52691666666666|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the precincts}}]] '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Nishihassaku-cho, [[:en:Midori-ku, Yokohama|Midori-ku, Yokohama]], Kanagawa Prefecture. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and the sixth shrine of [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]. Its historical classification under the modern shrine Ranking system was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine participates in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|1=Kurayami Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265601}} of [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]], and is counted among the "Six Great Shrines of Bushū" (武州六大明神).This includes {{ill|Ono Shrine (Tama)|ja|小野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Tama)|1=Ono Shrine (Tama)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464223}} in Tama City, Tokyo; {{ill|Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|ja|二宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|1=Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371507}} in Akiruno City, Tokyo; [[:en:Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|Hikawa Shrine (Omiya)]] in Omiya, Saitama; [[:en:Chichibu Shrine|Chichibu Shrine]] in Chichibu, Saitama; [[:en:Kanasana Shrine|Kanazawa Shrine]] in Kamikawa, Saitama; and Sugiyama Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}} === Associated Deities === * {{ill|Dainichi-ryūkei|ja|大日霊貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi-ryūkei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|太田命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} According to the [[:en:Shintōshū|Shintō-shū]], the shrine was historically called "Sugiyama Daimyōjin" (杉山大明神) with [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] as its Buddhist counterpart. Records from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] note the principal deity as a standing statue of Fudō Myōō.{{Cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 83, Tsuzuki District, Sugiyama Shrine|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763987/39}}}} In [[:en:1910|1910]], four nearby unranked shrines, including [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]], were merged with Sugiyama Shrine. == History == References to "Sugiyama Shrine" appear in the [[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihon Kōki]], while the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} lists it as the only official shrine of Tsuzuki District in Musashi Province. The shrine is also honored as one of the six "Six Shrines of Ōkunitama Shrine." The founding date is uncertain, but inscriptions on roof tiles and gravestones in the neighboring temple, Gokuraku-ji, suggest the shrine was relocated to its current location during the [[:en:Enpō|Enpō era]] (1673–1681). Before that, it stood 300 meters northwest at the base of a hill.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140819083559/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/musashi/bun/mus170201-01.html Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Survey of Shikinaisha) In [[:en:1649|1649]], the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} granted the shrine 5.6 koku of rice as stipends. In [[:en:1873|1873]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in [[:en:1910|1910]], it absorbed four neighboring shrines. In [[:en:1920|1920]], it was designated as a shrine eligible for official offerings. The current shrine building was rebuilt in [[:en:1982|1982]]. == Grounds == * '''Main Shrine:''' Built in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * '''Worship Hall:''' Built in [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style. * '''Chikara-ishi (Strength Stone):''' A boulder used for strength contests by the shrine's parishioners during the Edo period.Stone plaque in the precincts File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), haiden-2.jpg|Worship Hall File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho) 04.JPG|Chikara-ishi File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidaisha.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Sub-shrines == * '''Small Shrine:''' A small [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] structure enclosed in a thatched roof building. * '''Inari Shrine:''' A [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] shrine housed in a {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}} structure. == Festivals == * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan Festival|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:October 1|October 1]]: festival (formerly held on [[:en:September 15|September 15]]) * [[:en:November 23|November 23]]: [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] * [[:en:December 22|December 22]]: [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Ōharae]] Additionally, the shrine participates in the "Kurayami Festival" of [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]] in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|ja|府中市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuchū, Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Fuchū City|WD=Q226480}}, held from [[:en:May 3|May 3]] to [[:en:May 6|May 6]]. == Related Shrines == Sugiyama Shrine is one of many shrines of the same Tsuzuki-ku)|1=Sugiyama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17994298}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|1=Sugiyama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17994294}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|1=Sugiyama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17994298}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|1=Sugiyama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17994300}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho)}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=179&cd=1202084&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine (Kanagawa Shrine Office)] * [http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_10.php Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Akanedai.net) {{translated page|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)|version=103331334|comment=Imported [[sn:commons:Category:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho)]][[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11521945]] Wikidata name: Sugiyama jinjya [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Sugiyama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), haiden-2.jpg | location = 208 Miyamae, Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 35.5284 | longitude = 139.5269 | deity = {{ill|Isotakeru|WD=Q10881556}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor) debated
6th shrine of Musashi Province
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = Unknown | main_building_style = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festivals = [[:en:October 1|October 1]] | location = {{ill|Midori-ku|WD=Q1203373}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.52836111111111|N|139.52691666666666|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the precincts}}]] '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Nishihassaku-cho, [[:en:Midori-ku, Yokohama|Midori-ku, Yokohama]], Kanagawa Prefecture. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and the sixth shrine of [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]. Its historical classification under the modern shrine Ranking system was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine participates in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|1=Kurayami Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265601}} of [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]], and is counted among the "Six Great Shrines of Bushū" (武州六大明神).This includes {{ill|Ono Shrine (Tama)|ja|小野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Tama)|1=Ono Shrine (Tama)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464223}} in Tama City, Tokyo; {{ill|Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|ja|二宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|1=Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371507}} in Akiruno City, Tokyo; [[:en:Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|Hikawa Shrine (Omiya)]] in Omiya, Saitama; [[:en:Chichibu Shrine|Chichibu Shrine]] in Chichibu, Saitama; [[:en:Kanasana Shrine|Kanazawa Shrine]] in Kamikawa, Saitama; and Sugiyama Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}} === Associated Deities === * {{ill|Dainichi-ryūkei|ja|大日霊貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi-ryūkei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|太田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} According to the [[:en:Shintōshū|Shintō-shū]], the shrine was historically called "Sugiyama Daimyōjin" (杉山大明神) with [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] as its Buddhist counterpart. Records from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] note the principal deity as a standing statue of Fudō Myōō.{{Cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 83, Tsuzuki District, Sugiyama Shrine|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763987/39}}}} In [[:en:1910|1910]], four nearby unranked shrines, including [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]], were merged with Sugiyama Shrine. == History == References to "Sugiyama Shrine" appear in the [[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihon Kōki]], while the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} lists it as the only official shrine of Tsuzuki District in Musashi Province. The shrine is also honored as one of the six "Six Shrines of Ōkunitama Shrine." The founding date is uncertain, but inscriptions on roof tiles and gravestones in the neighboring temple, Gokuraku-ji, suggest the shrine was relocated to its current location during the [[:en:Enpō|Enpō era]] (1673–1681). Before that, it stood 300 meters northwest at the base of a hill.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140819083559/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/musashi/bun/mus170201-01.html Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Survey of Shikinaisha) In [[:en:1649|1649]], the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} granted the shrine 5.6 koku of rice as stipends. In [[:en:1873|1873]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in [[:en:1910|1910]], it absorbed four neighboring shrines. In [[:en:1920|1920]], it was designated as a shrine eligible for official offerings. The current shrine building was rebuilt in [[:en:1982|1982]]. == Grounds == * '''Main Shrine:''' Built in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * '''Worship Hall:''' Built in [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style. * '''Chikara-ishi (Strength Stone):''' A boulder used for strength contests by the shrine's parishioners during the Edo period.Stone plaque in the precincts File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), haiden-2.jpg|Worship Hall File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho) 04.JPG|Chikara-ishi File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidaisha.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Sub-shrines == * '''Small Shrine:''' A small [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] structure enclosed in a thatched roof building. * '''Inari Shrine:''' A [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] shrine housed in a {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}} structure. == Festivals == * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan Festival|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:October 1|October 1]]: festival (formerly held on [[:en:September 15|September 15]]) * [[:en:November 23|November 23]]: [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] * [[:en:December 22|December 22]]: [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Ōharae]] Additionally, the shrine participates in the "Kurayami Festival" of [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]] in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|ja|府中市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuchū, Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Fuchū City|WD=Q226480}}, held from [[:en:May 3|May 3]] to [[:en:May 6|May 6]]. == Related Shrines == Sugiyama Shrine is one of many shrines of the same name in Yokohama, including: * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|1=Sugiyama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17994294}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|1=Sugiyama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17994298}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|1=Sugiyama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17994300}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho)}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=179&cd=1202084&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine (Kanagawa Shrine Office)] * [http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_10.php Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Akanedai.net) {{translated page|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)|version=103331334|comment=Imported revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho)]][[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11521945]] Wikidata name: Sugiyama jinjya [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Sugiyama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), haiden-2.jpg | location = 208 Miyamae, Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 35.5284 | longitude = 139.5269 | deity = {{ill|Isotakeru|WD=Q10881556}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor) debated
6th shrine of Musashi Province
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = Unknown | main_building_style = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festivals = [[:en:October 1|October 1]] | location = {{ill|Midori-ku|WD=Q1203373}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.52836111111111|N|139.52691666666666|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the precincts}}]] '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Nishihassaku-cho, [[:en:Midori-ku, Yokohama|Midori-ku, Yokohama]], Kanagawa Prefecture. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and the sixth shrine of [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]. Its historical classification under the modern shrine Ranking system was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine participates in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|1=Kurayami Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265601}} of [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]], and is counted among the "Six Great Shrines of Bushū" (武州六大明神).This includes {{ill|Ono Shrine (Tama)|ja|小野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Tama)|1=Ono Shrine (Tama)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464223}} in Tama City, Tokyo; {{ill|Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|ja|二宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|1=Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371507}} in Akiruno City, Tokyo; [[:en:Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|Hikawa Shrine (Omiya)]] in Omiya, Saitama; [[:en:Chichibu Shrine|Chichibu Shrine]] in Chichibu, Saitama; [[:en:Kanasana Shrine|Kanazawa Shrine]] in Kamikawa, Saitama; and Sugiyama Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}} === Associated Deities === * {{ill|Dainichi-ryūkei|ja|大日霊貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi-ryūkei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|太田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} According to the [[:en:Shintōshū|Shintō-shū]], the shrine was historically called "Sugiyama Daimyōjin" (杉山大明神) with [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] as its Buddhist counterpart. Records from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] note the principal deity as a standing statue of Fudō Myōō.{{Cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 83, Tsuzuki District, Sugiyama Shrine|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763987/39}}}} In [[:en:1910|1910]], four nearby unranked shrines, including [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]], were merged with Sugiyama Shrine. == History == References to "Sugiyama Shrine" appear in the [[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihon Kōki]], while the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} lists it as the only official shrine of Tsuzuki District in Musashi Province. The shrine is also honored as one of the six "Six Shrines of Ōkunitama Shrine." The founding date is uncertain, but inscriptions on roof tiles and gravestones in the neighboring temple, Gokuraku-ji, suggest the shrine was relocated to its current location during the [[:en:Enpō|Enpō era]] (1673–1681). Before that, it stood 300 meters northwest at the base of a hill.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140819083559/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/musashi/bun/mus170201-01.html Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Survey of Shikinaisha) In [[:en:1649|1649]], the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} granted the shrine 5.6 koku of rice as stipends. In [[:en:1873|1873]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in [[:en:1910|1910]], it absorbed four neighboring shrines. In [[:en:1920|1920]], it was designated as a shrine eligible for official offerings. The current shrine building was rebuilt in [[:en:1982|1982]]. == Grounds == * '''Main Shrine:''' Built in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * '''Worship Hall:''' Built in [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style. * '''Chikara-ishi (Strength Stone):''' A boulder used for strength contests by the shrine's parishioners during the Edo period.Stone plaque in the precincts File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), haiden-2.jpg|Worship Hall File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho) 04.JPG|Chikara-ishi File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidaisha.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Sub-shrines == * '''Small Shrine:''' A small [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] structure enclosed in a thatched roof building. * '''Inari Shrine:''' A [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] shrine housed in a {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}} structure. == Festivals == * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan Festival|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:October 1|October 1]]: festival (formerly held on [[:en:September 15|September 15]]) * [[:en:November 23|November 23]]: [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] * [[:en:December 22|December 22]]: [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Ōharae]] Additionally, the shrine participates in the "Kurayami Festival" of [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]] in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|ja|府中市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuchū, Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Fuchū City|WD=Q226480}}, held from [[:en:May 3|May 3]] to [[:en:May 6|May 6]]. == Related Shrines == Sugiyama Shrine is one of many shrines of the same name in Yokohama, including: * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|1=Sugiyama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17994294}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|1=Sugiyama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17994298}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|1=Sugiyama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17994300}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho)}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=179&cd=1202084&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine (Kanagawa Shrine Office)] * [http://akanedai.net/sugiyama_10.php Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Akanedai.net) {{translated page|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)|version=103331334|comment=Imported It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and the sixth shrine of [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]. Its historical classification under the modern shrine Ranking system was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine participates in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|1=Kurayami Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265601}} of [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]], and is counted among the "Six Great Shrines of Bushū" (武州六大明神).This includes {{ill|Ono Shrine (Tama)|ja|小野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Tama)|1=Ono Shrine (Tama)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464223}} in Tama City, Tokyo; {{ill|Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|ja|二宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|1=Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371507}} in Akiruno City, Tokyo; [[:en:Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|Hikawa Shrine (Omiya)]] in Omiya, Saitama; [[:en:Chichibu Shrine|Chichibu Shrine]] in Chichibu, Saitama; [[:en:Kanasana Shrine|Kanazawa Shrine]] in Kamikawa, Saitama; and Sugiyama Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}} === Associated Deities === * {{ill|Dainichi-ryūkei|ja|大日霊貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi-ryūkei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|太田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} According to the [[:en:Shintōshū|Shintō-shū]], the shrine was historically called "Sugiyama Daimyōjin" (杉山大明神) with [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] as its Buddhist counterpart. Records from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] note the principal deity as a standing statue of Fudō Myōō.{{Cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 83, Tsuzuki District, Sugiyama Shrine|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763987/39}}}} In [[:en:1910|1910]], four nearby unranked shrines, including [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]], were merged with Sugiyama Shrine. == History == References to "Sugiyama Shrine" appear in the [[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihon Kōki]], while the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} lists it as the only official shrine of Tsuzuki District in Musashi Province. The shrine is also honored as one of the six "Six Shrines of Ōkunitama Shrine." The founding date is uncertain, but inscriptions on roof tiles and gravestones in the neighboring temple, Gokuraku-ji, suggest the shrine was relocated to its current location during the [[:en:Enpō|Enpō era]] (1673–1681). Before that, it stood 300 meters northwest at the base of a hill.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140819083559/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/musashi/bun/mus170201-01.html Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Survey of Shikinaisha) In [[:en:1649|1649]], the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} granted the shrine 5.6 koku of rice as stipends. In [[:en:1873|1873]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in [[:en:1910|1910]], it absorbed four neighboring shrines. In [[:en:1920|1920]], it was designated as a shrine eligible for official offerings. The current shrine building was rebuilt in [[:en:1982|1982]]. == Grounds == * '''Main Shrine:''' Built in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * '''Worship Hall:''' Built in [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style. * '''Chikara-ishi (Strength Stone):''' A boulder used for strength contests by the shrine's parishioners during the Edo period.Stone plaque in the precincts 6th shrine of Musashi Province
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = Unknown | main_building_style = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festivals = [[:en:October 1|October 1]] | location = {{ill|Midori-ku|WD=Q1203373}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.52836111111111|N|139.52691666666666|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the precincts}}]] '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Nishihassaku-cho, [[:en:Midori-ku, Yokohama|Midori-ku, Yokohama]], Kanagawa Prefecture. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and the sixth shrine of [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]. Its historical classification under the modern shrine Ranking system was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine participates in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|1=Kurayami Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265601}} of [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]], and is counted among the "Six Great Shrines of Bushū" (武州六大明神).This includes {{ill|Ono Shrine (Tama)|ja|小野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Tama)|1=Ono Shrine (Tama)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464223}} in Tama City, Tokyo; {{ill|Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|ja|二宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|1=Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371507}} in Akiruno City, Tokyo; [[:en:Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|Hikawa Shrine (Omiya)]] in Omiya, Saitama; [[:en:Chichibu Shrine|Chichibu Shrine]] in Chichibu, Saitama; [[:en:Kanasana Shrine|Kanazawa Shrine]] in Kamikawa, Saitama; and Sugiyama Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}} === Associated Deities === * {{ill|Dainichi-ryūkei|ja|大日霊貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi-ryūkei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|太田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} According to the [[:en:Shintōshū|Shintō-shū]], the shrine was historically called "Sugiyama Daimyōjin" (杉山大明神) with [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] as its Buddhist counterpart. Records from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] note the principal deity as a standing statue of Fudō Myōō.{{Cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 83, Tsuzuki District, Sugiyama Shrine|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763987/39}}}} In [[:en:1910|1910]], four nearby unranked shrines, including [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]], were merged with Sugiyama Shrine. == History == References to "Sugiyama Shrine" appear in the [[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihon Kōki]], while the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} lists it as the only official shrine of Tsuzuki District in Musashi Province. The shrine is also honored as one of the six "Six Shrines of Ōkunitama Shrine." The founding date is uncertain, but inscriptions on roof tiles and gravestones in the neighboring temple, Gokuraku-ji, suggest the shrine was relocated to its current location during the [[:en:Enpō|Enpō era]] (1673–1681). Before that, it stood 300 meters northwest at the base of a hill.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140819083559/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/musashi/bun/mus170201-01.html Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Survey of Shikinaisha) In [[:en:1649|1649]], the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} granted the shrine 5.6 koku of rice as stipends. In [[:en:1873|1873]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in [[:en:1910|1910]], it absorbed four neighboring shrines. In [[:en:1920|1920]], it was designated as a shrine eligible for official offerings. The current shrine building was rebuilt in [[:en:1982|1982]]. == Grounds == * '''Main Shrine:''' Built in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * '''Worship Hall:''' Built in [[:en:East Asian Records from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] note the principal deity as a standing statue of Fudō Myōō.{{Cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 83, Tsuzuki District, Sugiyama Shrine|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763987/39}}}} In [[:en:1910|1910]], four nearby unranked shrines, including [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]], were merged with Sugiyama Shrine. == History == References to "Sugiyama Shrine" appear in the [[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihon Kōki]], while the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} lists it as the only official shrine of Tsuzuki District in Musashi Province. The shrine is also honored as one of the six "Six Shrines of Ōkunitama Shrine." The founding date is uncertain, but inscriptions on roof tiles and gravestones in the neighboring temple, Gokuraku-ji, suggest the shrine was relocated to its current location during the [[:en:Enpō|Enpō era]] (1673–1681). Before that, it stood 300 meters northwest at the base of a hill.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140819083559/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/musashi/bun/mus170201-01.html Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Survey of Shikinaisha) In [[:en:1649|1649]], the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} granted the shrine 5.6 koku of rice as stipends. In [[:en:1873|1873]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in [[:en:1910|1910]], it absorbed four neighboring shrines. In [[:en:1920|1920]], it was designated as a shrine eligible for official offerings. The current shrine building was rebuilt in [[:en:1982|1982]]. == Grounds == * '''Main Shrine:''' Built in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * '''Worship Hall:''' Built in [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style. * '''Chikara-ishi (Strength Stone):''' A boulder used for strength contests by the shrine's parishioners during the Edo period.Stone plaque in the precincts Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidaisha.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Sub-shrines == * '''Small Shrine:''' A small [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] structure enclosed in a thatched roof building. * '''Inari Shrine:''' A [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] shrine housed in a participates in the "Kurayami Festival" of [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]] in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|ja|府中市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuchū, Tokyo|comment=changed title based on 6th shrine of Musashi Province
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = Unknown | main_building_style = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festivals = [[:en:October 1|October 1]] | location = {{ill|Midori-ku|WD=Q1203373}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.52836111111111|N|139.52691666666666|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the precincts}}]] '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Nishihassaku-cho, [[:en:Midori-ku, Yokohama|Midori-ku, Yokohama]], Kanagawa Prefecture. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and the sixth shrine of [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]. Its historical classification under the modern shrine Ranking system was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine participates in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|1=Kurayami Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265601}} of [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]], and is counted among the "Six Great Shrines of Bushū" (武州六大明神).This includes {{ill|Ono Shrine (Tama)|ja|小野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Tama)|1=Ono Shrine (Tama)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464223}} in Tama City, Tokyo; {{ill|Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|ja|二宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|1=Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371507}} in Akiruno City, Tokyo; [[:en:Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|Hikawa Shrine (Omiya)]] in Omiya, Saitama; [[:en:Chichibu Shrine|Chichibu Shrine]] in Chichibu, Saitama; [[:en:Kanasana Shrine|Kanazawa Shrine]] in Kamikawa, Saitama; and Sugiyama Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}} === Associated Deities === * {{ill|Dainichi-ryūkei|ja|大日霊貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi-ryūkei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|太田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} According to the [[:en:Shintōshū|Shintō-shū]], the shrine was historically called "Sugiyama Daimyōjin" (杉山大明神) with [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] as its Buddhist counterpart. Records from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] note the principal deity as a standing statue of Fudō Myōō.{{Cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 83, Tsuzuki District, Sugiyama Shrine|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763987/39}}}} In [[:en:1910|1910]], four nearby unranked shrines, including [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]], were merged with Sugiyama Shrine. == History == References to "Sugiyama Shrine" appear in the [[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihon Kōki]], while the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} lists it as the only official shrine of Tsuzuki District in Musashi Province. The shrine is also honored as one of the six "Six Shrines of Ōkunitama Shrine." The founding date is uncertain, but inscriptions on roof tiles and gravestones in the neighboring temple, Gokuraku-ji, suggest the shrine was relocated to its current location during the [[:en:Enpō|Enpō era]] (1673–1681). Before that, it stood 300 meters northwest at the base of a hill.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140819083559/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/musashi/bun/mus170201-01.html Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Survey of Shikinaisha) In [[:en:1649|1649]], the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} granted the shrine 5.6 koku of rice as stipends. In [[:en:1873|1873]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in [[:en:1910|1910]], it absorbed four neighboring shrines. In [[:en:1920|1920]], it was designated as a shrine eligible for official offerings. The current shrine building was rebuilt in [[:en:1982|1982]]. == Grounds == * '''Main Shrine:''' Built in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * '''Worship Hall:''' Built in [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style. * '''Chikara-ishi (Strength Stone):''' A boulder used for strength contests by the shrine's parishioners during the Edo period.Stone plaque in the precincts File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), haiden-2.jpg|Worship Hall File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho) 04.JPG|Chikara-ishi File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidaisha.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Sub-shrines == * '''Small Shrine:''' A small [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] structure enclosed in a thatched roof building. * '''Inari Shrine:''' A [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] shrine housed in a {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}} structure. == Festivals == * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan Festival|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:October 1|October 1]]: festival (formerly held on [[:en:September 15|September 15]]) * [[:en:November 23|November 23]]: [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] * [[:en:December 22|December 22]]: [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Ōharae]] Additionally, the shrine participates in the "Kurayami Festival" of [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]] in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|ja|府中市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuchū, Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Fuchū City|WD=Q226480}}, held from [[:en:May 3|May 3]] to [[:en:May 6|May 6]]. == Related Shrines == Sugiyama Shrine is one of many shrines of the same name in Yokohama, including: * [[sn:commons:Category:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho)]][[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11521945]] Wikidata name: Sugiyama jinjya [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Sugiyama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), haiden-2.jpg | location = 208 Miyamae, Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 35.5284 | longitude = 139.5269 | deity = {{ill|Isotakeru|WD=Q10881556}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor) debated
6th shrine of Musashi Province
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = Unknown | main_building_style = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festivals = [[:en:October 1|October 1]] | location = {{ill|Midori-ku|WD=Q1203373}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.52836111111111|N|139.52691666666666|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the precincts}}]] '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Nishihassaku-cho, [[:en:Midori-ku, Yokohama|Midori-ku, Yokohama]], Kanagawa Prefecture. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and the sixth shrine of [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]. Its historical classification under the modern shrine Ranking system was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine participates in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|1=Kurayami Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265601}} of [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]], and is counted among the "Six Great Shrines of Bushū" (武州六大明神).This includes {{ill|Ono Shrine (Tama)|ja|小野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Tama)|1=Ono Shrine (Tama)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464223}} in Tama City, Tokyo; {{ill|Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|ja|二宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|1=Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371507}} in Akiruno City, Tokyo; [[:en:Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|Hikawa Shrine (Omiya)]] in Omiya, Saitama; [[:en:Chichibu Shrine|Chichibu Shrine]] in Chichibu, Saitama; [[:en:Kanasana Shrine|Kanazawa Shrine]] in Kamikawa, Saitama; and Sugiyama Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}} === Associated Deities === * {{ill|Dainichi-ryūkei|ja|大日霊貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi-ryūkei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|太田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} According to the [[:en:Shintōshū|Shintō-shū]], the shrine was historically called "Sugiyama Daimyōjin" (杉山大明神) with [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] as its Buddhist counterpart. Records from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] note the principal deity as a standing statue of Fudō Myōō.{{Cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 83, Tsuzuki District, Sugiyama Shrine|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763987/39}}}} In [[:en:1910|1910]], four nearby unranked shrines, including [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]], were merged with Sugiyama Shrine. == History == References to "Sugiyama Shrine" appear in the [[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihon Kōki]], while the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} lists it as the only official shrine of Tsuzuki District in Musashi Province. The shrine is also honored as one of the six "Six Shrines of Ōkunitama Shrine." The founding date is uncertain, but inscriptions on roof tiles and gravestones in the neighboring temple, Gokuraku-ji, suggest the shrine was relocated to its current location during the [[:en:Enpō|Enpō era]] (1673–1681). Before that, it stood 300 meters northwest at the base of a hill.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140819083559/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/musashi/bun/mus170201-01.html Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Survey of Shikinaisha) In [[:en:1649|1649]], the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} granted the shrine 5.6 koku of rice as stipends. In [[:en:1873|1873]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in [[:en:1910|1910]], it absorbed four neighboring shrines. In [[:en:1920|1920]], it was designated as a shrine eligible for official offerings. The current shrine building was rebuilt in [[:en:1982|1982]]. == Grounds == * '''Main Shrine:''' Built in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * '''Worship Hall:''' Built in [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style. * '''Chikara-ishi (Strength Stone):''' A boulder used for strength contests by the shrine's parishioners during the Edo period.Stone plaque in the precincts File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), haiden-2.jpg|Worship Hall File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho) 04.JPG|Chikara-ishi File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidaisha.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Sub-shrines == * '''Small Shrine:''' A small [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] structure enclosed in a thatched roof building. * '''Inari Shrine:''' A [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] shrine housed in a {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}} structure. == Festivals == * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan Festival|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:October 1|October 1]]: festival (formerly held on [[:en:September 15|September 15]]) * [[:en:November 23|November 23]]: [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] * [[:en:December 22|December 22]]: [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Ōharae]] Additionally, the shrine participates in the "Kurayami Festival" of [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]] in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|ja|府中市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuchū, Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Fuchū City|WD=Q226480}}, held from [[:en:May 3|May 3]] to [[:en:May 6|May 6]]. == Related Shrines == Sugiyama Shrine is one of many shrines of the same name in Yokohama, including: * wikidata|WD=Q17994298}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|1=Sugiyama Shrine|comment=changed title [[sn:commons:Category:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho)]][[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11521945]] Wikidata name: Sugiyama jinjya [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Sugiyama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), haiden-2.jpg | location = 208 Miyamae, Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 35.5284 | longitude = 139.5269 | deity = {{ill|Isotakeru|WD=Q10881556}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor) debated
6th shrine of Musashi Province
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = Unknown | main_building_style = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | festivals = [[:en:October 1|October 1]] | location = {{ill|Midori-ku|WD=Q1203373}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.52836111111111|N|139.52691666666666|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the precincts}}]] '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Nishihassaku-cho, [[:en:Midori-ku, Yokohama|Midori-ku, Yokohama]], Kanagawa Prefecture. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and the sixth shrine of [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]. Its historical classification under the modern shrine Ranking system was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine participates in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|1=Kurayami Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265601}} of [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]], and is counted among the "Six Great Shrines of Bushū" (武州六大明神).This includes {{ill|Ono Shrine (Tama)|ja|小野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Tama)|1=Ono Shrine (Tama)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464223}} in Tama City, Tokyo; {{ill|Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|ja|二宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|1=Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371507}} in Akiruno City, Tokyo; [[:en:Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|Hikawa Shrine (Omiya)]] in Omiya, Saitama; [[:en:Chichibu Shrine|Chichibu Shrine]] in Chichibu, Saitama; [[:en:Kanasana Shrine|Kanazawa Shrine]] in Kamikawa, Saitama; and Sugiyama Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}} === Associated Deities === * {{ill|Dainichi-ryūkei|ja|大日霊貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi-ryūkei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|太田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} According to the [[:en:Shintōshū|Shintō-shū]], the shrine was historically called "Sugiyama Daimyōjin" (杉山大明神) with [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] as its Buddhist counterpart. Records from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] note the principal deity as a standing statue of Fudō Myōō.{{Cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 83, Tsuzuki District, Sugiyama Shrine|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763987/39}}}} In [[:en:1910|1910]], four nearby unranked shrines, including [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]], were merged with Sugiyama Shrine. == History == References to "Sugiyama Shrine" appear in the [[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihon Kōki]], while the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} lists it as the only official shrine of Tsuzuki District in Musashi Province. The shrine is also honored as one of the six "Six Shrines of Ōkunitama Shrine." The founding date is uncertain, but inscriptions on roof tiles and gravestones in the neighboring temple, Gokuraku-ji, suggest the shrine was relocated to its current location during the [[:en:Enpō|Enpō era]] (1673–1681). Before that, it stood 300 meters northwest at the base of a hill.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140819083559/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/musashi/bun/mus170201-01.html Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Survey of Shikinaisha) In [[:en:1649|1649]], the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} granted the shrine 5.6 koku of rice as stipends. In [[:en:1873|1873]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in [[:en:1910|1910]], it absorbed four neighboring shrines. In [[:en:1920|1920]], it was designated as a shrine eligible for official offerings. The current shrine building was rebuilt in [[:en:1982|1982]]. == Grounds == * '''Main Shrine:''' Built in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * '''Worship Hall:''' Built in [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style. * '''Chikara-ishi (Strength Stone):''' A boulder used for strength contests by the shrine's parishioners during the Edo period.Stone plaque in the precincts Its historical classification under the modern shrine Ranking system was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine participates in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|1=Kurayami Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265601}} of [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]], and is counted among the "Six Great Shrines of Bushū" (武州六大明神).This includes {{ill|Ono Shrine (Tama)|ja|小野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Tama)|1=Ono Shrine (Tama)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464223}} in Tama City, Tokyo; {{ill|Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|ja|二宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|1=Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371507}} in Akiruno City, Tokyo; [[:en:Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|Hikawa Shrine (Omiya)]] in Omiya, Saitama; [[:en:Chichibu Shrine|Chichibu Shrine]] in Chichibu, Saitama; [[:en:Kanasana Shrine|Kanazawa Shrine]] in Kamikawa, Saitama; and Sugiyama Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}} === Associated Deities === * {{ill|Dainichi-ryūkei|ja|大日霊貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi-ryūkei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|太田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} According to the [[:en:Shintōshū|Shintō-shū]], the shrine was historically called "Sugiyama Daimyōjin" (杉山大明神) with [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] as its Buddhist counterpart. Records from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] note the principal deity as a standing statue of Fudō Myōō.{{Cite book|ja|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 83, Tsuzuki District, Sugiyama Shrine|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Interior|date=1884-06|id={{NDLJP|763987/39}}}} In [[:en:1910|1910]], four nearby unranked shrines, including [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]], were merged with Sugiyama Shrine. == History == References to "Sugiyama Shrine" appear in the [[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku Nihon Kōki]], while the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} lists it as the only official shrine of Tsuzuki District in Musashi Province. The shrine is also honored as one of the six "Six Shrines of Ōkunitama Shrine." The founding date is uncertain, but inscriptions on roof tiles and gravestones in the neighboring temple, Gokuraku-ji, suggest the shrine was relocated to its current location during the [[:en:Enpō|Enpō era]] (1673–1681). Before that, it stood 300 meters northwest at the base of a hill.[https://web.archive.org/web/20140819083559/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/musashi/bun/mus170201-01.html Sugiyama Shrine (Nishihassaku)] (Survey of Shikinaisha) In [[:en:1649|1649]], the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} granted the shrine 5.6 koku of rice as stipends. In [[:en:1873|1873]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in [[:en:1910|1910]], it absorbed four neighboring shrines. In [[:en:1920|1920]], it was designated as a shrine eligible for official offerings. The current shrine building was rebuilt in [[:en:1982|1982]]. == Grounds == * '''Main Shrine:''' Built in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * '''Worship Hall:''' Built in [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style. * '''Chikara-ishi (Strength Stone):''' A boulder used for strength contests by the shrine's parishioners during the Edo period.Stone plaque in the precincts File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), haiden-2.jpg|Worship Hall File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho) 04.JPG|Chikara-ishi File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), keidaisha.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Sub-shrines == * '''Small Shrine:''' A small [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] structure enclosed in a thatched roof building. * '''Inari Shrine:''' A [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] shrine housed in a {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}} structure. == Festivals == * [[:en:January 1|January 1]]: {{ill|Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan Festival|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:October 1|October 1]]: festival (formerly held on [[:en:September 15|September 15]]) * [[:en:November 23|November 23]]: [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] * [[:en:December 22|December 22]]: [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Ōharae]] Additionally, the shrine participates in the "Kurayami Festival" of [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]] in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|ja|府中市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuchū, Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Fuchū City|WD=Q226480}}, held from [[:en:May 3|May 3]] to [[:en:May 6|May 6]]. == Related Shrines == Sugiyama Shrine is one of many shrines of the same name in Yokohama, including: * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市港北区新吉田町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida)|1=Sugiyama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17994294}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Chigasaki, Tsuzuki-ku)|1=Sugiyama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17994298}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine (Nakagawa, Tsuzuki-ku)|1=Sugiyama Shrine|comment=changed title
