[[co:commons:Category:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho)]][[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)]] [[co:commons:Category:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Nishi-hasaku-cho)]][[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)]] [[ja:杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Sugiyama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), haiden-2.jpg | location = 208 Miyamae, Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 35.5284 | longitude = 139.5269 | deity = {{ill|Isotakeru|WD=Q10881556|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor) debated
6th shrine of Musashi Province
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = Unknown | main_building_style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | festivals = {{ill|October 1|en|October 1|WD=Q2913|lt=October 1|1=October 1}} | location = {{ill|Midori-ku|WD=Q1203373|1=Midori-ku, Yokohama}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.52836111111111|N|139.52691666666666|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} }} [[File:Sugiyama-jinja (Nishihassaku-cho, Midori-ku, Yokohama), entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the precincts}}]] '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Nishihassaku-cho, {{ill|Midori-ku, Yokohama|en|Midori-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1203373|lt=Midori-ku, Yokohama|1=Midori-ku, Yokohama}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. It is a debated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and the sixth shrine of {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province|1=Musashi Province}}. Its historical classification under the modern shrine Ranking system was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine participates in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|1=Kurayami Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265601}} of {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|en|Ōkunitama Shrine|WD=Q611678|lt=Ōkunitama Shrine|1=Ōkunitama Shrine}}, and is counted among the "Six Great Shrines of Bushū" (武州六大明神).This includes {{ill|Ono Shrine (Tama)|ja|小野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Tama)|1=Q11464223|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464223}} in Tama City, Tokyo; {{ill|Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|ja|二宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Shrine (Akiruno)|1=Q11371507|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371507}} in Akiruno City, Tokyo; {{ill|Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|en|Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|WD=Q703633|lt=Hikawa Shrine (Omiya)|1=Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)}} in Omiya, Saitama; {{ill|Chichibu Shrine|en|Chichibu Shrine|WD=Q2963366|lt=Chichibu Shrine|1=Chichibu Shrine}} in Chichibu, Saitama; {{ill|Kanasana Shrine|en|Kanasana Shrine|WD=Q11647974|lt=Kanazawa Shrine|1=Kanasana Shrine}} in Kamikawa, Saitama; and Sugiyama Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakeru-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}} === Associated Deities === * {{ill|Dainichi-ryūkei|ja|大日霊貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi-ryūkei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Mikoto|ja|太田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} According to the {{ill|Shintōshū|en|Shintōshū|WD=Q2616931|lt=Shintō-shū|1=Shintōshū}}, the shrine was historically called "Sugiyama Daimyōjin" (杉山大明神) with {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}} as its Buddhist counterpart. The founding date is uncertain, but inscriptions on roof tiles and gravestones in the neighboring temple, Gokuraku-ji, suggest the shrine was relocated to its current location during the {{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|WD=Q1145735|lt=Enpō era|1=Enpō}} (1673–1681). {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|en|Ōharae-shiki|WD=Q11261910|lt=Ōharae|1=Ōharae-shiki}} Additionally, the shrine participates in the "Kurayami Festival" of {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|en|Ōkunitama {{ill|May 3|en|May 3|WD=Q2547|lt=May 3|1=May 3}} to {{ill|May 6|en|May 6|WD=Q2552|lt=May 6|1=May 6}}. == Related Shrines == Sugiyama Shrine is one of many shrines of the same name in Yokohama,
